Company code 606 – to be added to IP box and AA S4 is required to be set up.​
Company code 241 to be added to CFIN only.​
Company code 724 – to be deleted from IF and keep CFIN only.​
Company code 604/622/645/405/879/429/735/736/756/334 – to be deleted from IP and keep them available in CFIN only.  To enable non-leading ledger for 429​
Rationale for the Change:
Legal Entity-776​​
LE776, Intel China Limited, was categorized as a “mixed entity”. It holds subsidiaries & employees that are IF and IP in nature. The entity was planned to be segregated to an IF company and an IP company via a demerger with LE776 being an IF Entity.  The entity had been set up in S4 as IF. ​​
Now demerger is not going to happen. Given 776 is pre-dominantly IP LE, it's more appropriate to move it to IP instance.​​

Legal Entity-390​
Nature of entity is predominant IP Entity but holds mixed IF and IP subsidiaries. ​
The original POR proposed by KPMG was to demerge LE390; with the existing entity being an IF Entity holding the Israel Fab. ​
Subsequently, to minimized impact to LE390 Employees, the plan was revised so that LE390 will retain its employees and hold IP subsidiaries instead of the Fab. ​

Legal Entity – 606​
Legal entity 606 (Mobileye) is a non-integrated entities maintained in CFIN only.  However, asset ledger will be required to manage intangible assets under 606.  Asset ledger is not available in CFIN, thus, it needs to be set up in IP to leverage asset ledger.​

Legal Entity –241​​
Legal Entity 241 was excluded from CFIN/S4 build due to expected liquidation by 2024. The liquidation team now forecasts that 241 will not be closed by 2024. Therefore, we need to create Entity 241 in CFIN to ensure financial balance completeness.​

Legal Entity –724​
Currently designated as IF entity, it was the China NAND Fab until the sale of the NAND business to SK Hynix.​
After the 1st closing in 2021, the entity has a handful of Employees and activities and is designated as IF/CFIN​
Entity expected to be fully dormant after the 2nd closure in Q3’25 and will be liquidated.   Thus, keep it in CFIN is sufficient.​

Legal Entity –604/622/645/405/879/429/735/736/756/334​
Currently designated as IP entities, they are on liquidation roadmap and only need to be in CFIN for liquidation capability.  With R3 being pushed out, LE COE team now has more confidence to close these legal entities before R3.​

For company code 429, non-leading ledger needs to be enabled as it is in scope of UK consolidation.  Though it is expected to be liquidated before R3, with GP replacing BCS as system of record in R2, we will still need 429 in non-leading ledger to ensure accurate UK consolidation
Notes from Core Leadership Review [Stage: Approved to proceed with R1/R2 work

Change Title*: Match Trade Flows to Physical Flows
Change Description: Change how we support tax requirements and associated trade flows.  Current design is that each time a manufacturing legal entity completes work and ships the value added is transferred to the order taking entity (US or Singapore).  This design creates significant complexity in both S/4 and Blue Yonder as well as boundary apps. The proposal is to modify the trade flows so that value transfers with the physical flow - i.e., to the next manufacturing site - and only transfers to the order taking entity prior to shipment to the customer.  This removes significant complexity in S/4, in BY, and in boundary apps
Rationale for the Change: Reduced complexity in S/4, BY, and boundary apps from simplified trade flow
Notes from Core Leadership Review [Stage: Approved

Change Title*: Company code move to different S4 instance 390/724/604/622/645/606
Change Description:
Company code 390 - to be moved from IF to IP
Company code 724 – to be deleted from IF and keep CFIN only.
Company code 604/622/645 – to be deleted from IP and make them available in CFIN only.
Company code 606 – to be added to IP box
Rationale for the Change:
Legal Entity-390
Nature of entity is predominant IP Entity but holds mixed IF and IP subsidiaries. 
The original POR proposed by KPMG was to demerge LE390; with the existing entity being an IF Entity holding the Israel Fab. 
Subsequently, to minimized impact to LE390 Employees,  and reassessment by KPMG, the plan was revised so that LE390 will retain its employees and hold IP subsidiaries instead of the Fab. 
Legal Entity –724
Currently designated as IF entity, it was the China NAND Fab until the sale of the NAND business to SK Hynix.
After the 1st closing in 2021, the entity has a handful of Employees and activities and is designated as IF/CFIN
Entity expected to be fully dormant after the 2nd closure in Q3’25 and will be liquidated. 
Legal Entity –604/622/645
Currently designated as IP entities, they are on liquidation roadmap and only need to be in CFIN for liquidation capability.
Legal Entity – 606
Both 606 (Mobileye) are non-integrated entities maintained in CFIN only.  However, asset ledger will be required to manage intangible assets under these 2 company codes.  Asset ledger is not available in CFIN, thus, they need to be added to IP to leverage asset ledger.
Comments for Rejection/Deferral: All details of this CR was combined with CR0051

Change Title*: Sales Org Inherited Pricing
Change Description:
When IPAR/MyDeals Interface sends price to S4 for AM01/AP01/EU01/JP01 sales org combinations in the payload, S4 program need to read the data in the file and create(multiply) pricing records for all S4 sales organization combinations. 

For example, if prices are received for AM01, then prices must be created for all AM* sales orgs in S4.AM* sales orgs are AM01,AM03,AM10,AM13 and AM15.
Prices for AP01 received: Prices must be created for AP* sales orgs, AP* sales orgs are AP01,AP03,AP21, AP57
Prices for EU01 received: Prices must be created for EU* sales orgs, EU* sales orgs are EU01,EU02
Prices for JP01 received: Prices must be created for JP* sales orgs, JP* sales orgs are JP01
Sales org reference table in S4 is TVKO.
This CR should be implemented for Interfaces OTCI0081 (Pricing feed from IPAR to S4)and OTCI0082(Pricing feed from My Deals to S4). The scenarios of Upload ad Expire scenarios should be considered here
Rationale for the Change: IPAR/MyDeals load prices to S4 for main sales organizations(AM01, AP01,JP01, EU01) only but sales orders are created in S4 for super distis like AM03, AP03, EU02. In S4, we need to have prices for all super disti’s so, that during sales order creation the price can be determined automatically from the respective sales organization pricing condition tables

Change Title*: GRC Landscape Strategy Change
Change Description:
In the current landscape strategy (slide 3), Production GRC provisions to all SAP systems (both production and non-production). ​

Change Title*: BTP Integration suite Vs Mulesoft
Change Description: We suggest utilyzing BTP's integration for integrations across the SAP ecosystem at Intel over Mulesoft, not just for SAP to SAP integrations but also for SAP to non-SAP integrations.
Rationale for the Change:
Mulesoft lacks standard adapters, leading to more custom development. 
Here are a few examples: Workday & Salesforce adapters into S/4HANA was missing in Mulesoft but were available in the BTP Integration suite. 
TPM on Integration suite can reduce EDI integration development time considerably.
Event Mesh for the O/B API integrations can help with real-time integrations but is missing in Mulesoft.

Change Title*: Deploy CALM alongside other tools for a smooth running Operations Control Center
Change Description: SAP believes Intel should pursue the implementation of best-practice, best-run Build & Run operations. SAP CALM alongisde SAP Solution Manager, Signavio and other non-SAP applications are recommended to be the foundation of the implementation of SAP's best practice Operations Control Center. We recommend having CALM in the landscape as soon as possible, ideally before testing of IAO starts. So that project operational data can begin to get captured.
Rationale for the Change: The combination of SAP CALM, Solution Manager and Signavio offers capabilities that can ensure "SAP Application and Business Process Operations" can be seamlessly monitored for not only KTLO but also be continously improved. These tools will also set the path for clean-core adherence & transport management in the short-term and help Intel's SAP applications become cloud-deployment ready through an establishment of DevOps. We are suggesting Solution Manager alongside CALM (both Application lifecycle Management tools) is that Intel gets complete ALM capabilities given CALM is an ALM tool for a cloud environment and Intel's SAP systems would be on-premise.

The ACS warranty management prepackaged soluton offers: 
Pre-configured functionality such as integration to PM processes, core waranty management foundation solution on which further functionality can be built. 
The solutio cuts down implementation timeline and reduces the solution risk significantly.

Change Title*: BP role Vs New BP for Treasury
Change Description:
Use a new BP role functionality within an existing BP instead of creating separate BPs for suppliers who are also customers and vice versa.  Combine Treasury Intercompany BPs that are similarly related. 
KDD Reference: KDDDAT002B
Rationale for the Change: One of the pillars of "Clean Core Standards" is clean and streamlined (harmonized, free of duplication) Master Data. One of the early phase 0 decisions for IAO was to seperate suppliers and customers into seperate Business Partners. This decision in return now forces the addition of another BP for Treasury Intercompany BP's. For future optimizations aiming at better Master Data quality and BP Harmonization to improve clean core standards, we recommend to harmonize those various BP's and use the BP roles in one BP instead of a seperate BP, if the entity reflected by the BP is the same.

Change Title*: Limited use of Std QM in various towers. For Eg: Quality inspection for RMAs is missing.
Change Description: The OSC/CL’s recent decision (Sept. 2024) to include SAP QM in scope is a positive move. SAP is delighted to endorse this plan.  SAP recommends that IAO utilize the SAP QM module to its fullest extent.  Intel will derive value from SAP’s  best in class solution (e.g. consider using QM in S/4 HANA manufacturing processes).
Rationale for the Change:
Various manufacturing processes require products to be inspected, stored in a given location to be dispositioned, and then released into available inventory or scrapped. Quality Management has embedded functionality to create a quality inspection lot, record inspection results, and release stock into the respective stock status (unrestricted, blocked, scrap). 
Using SAP QM even for SAP Manufacturing processes will help Intel retire the corresponding boundary apps, reducing the TCO through reduced RICEFWs and decreased licensing & maintenance costs for boundary app(s).

Change Title*: AI Capabilities and innovation supported by a cloud deployment
Change Description: Consider moving to the Cloud under RISE with SAP to have access to cutting edge AI innovation. Moreover, using BTP, is a low hanging fruit, especially for getting access to all the innovation in the AI space.
Rationale for the Change: SAP announced in 2023 that it will, going forward,  offer innovations only on cloud deployments that are either on Public cloud or private cloud. With AI, especially GenerativeAI, slowly becoming the cornerstone for competitive edge, missing out on such innovations can get tricky. Overall, out of a total of 203 AI use cases available as of now, only 103 use cases, mainly in the situation handling space, are available to Intel (They are embedded in SAP S/4HANA). Only 6 of those are in the Gen/AI space. 92 use cases are not available to Intel as they are available only on SAP BTP.

Change Title*: SAP Build Process automation for Workflow, RPA, decision management, process visibility and embedded AI capabilities
Change Description: Utilize SAP Build process automation, a SAP BTP app, for your workflow, automation, and low-/no-code citizen (End users) development, utilizing standard API's for integration with S/4HANA
Rationale for the Change:
Many processes at Intel require workflows mainly for approvals and other automation customization (wrapper programs for mass processing, bundling functionalities into more central user interfaces, etc.). Most of these functionalities are introduced in the project as custom coding (mimicking existing ECC6.0 custom programs). 
However, the "SAP Clean Core" imperatives and "Digital Solution" strategies foresee concepts, where core functionality resides in S4, while specific enhancements and individualization are achieved outside of, but closely interacting with, S4. The BTP app "SAP Build" with its capabilities for Process Automation, Bot creation and deployment, workflow management and low-/no-code citizen developer functionality can entirely replace the current automation related customizations at Intel and further enhance and improve processes as well as infuse those with predelivered automation functions, and intelligent functions as well as detailed process intelligence reporting of exceptions and deviations. These capabilities will further be expanded by SAP "outside" but highly integrated with S4HANA. SAP Build on BTP and its capabilities communicate with S4HANA via standard API's. RICEFW ID 2170, 2169 and many more related to BOT creations could have been eliminated by using SAP Build process automation.
Month of Approval: Missing Review Date for Approval Level

Change Title*: Utilizing SAP's AATP functionality in the IP S/4HANA box.
Change Description: We suggest that for IP S/4HANA, Intel should consider utilizing SAP's AATP functionality instead of relying on BY for order promising. This is not applicable for IF, which is a MTO (Make-To-Order) scenario. For IP, AATP + BOP within S/4HANA can be the way to go.
Rationale for the Change: Since IP does not have manufacturing at all, and since IP runs mostly on an MTS ( Make to stock) scenario, there may be no value in using BY for available-to-promise functionality. Rather Standard SAP-AATP with BOP ( Back order processing) provides way more flexibility with even a provision for manual reallocation when needed. Overall, this will help reduce integrations with BY.

Change Title*: IRC tool Vs SAP EMS
Change Description: SAP-EMS is currently only being set up for Physical products related warranties but not for software entitlments. We suggest extending the use of SAP EMS for software entitlements.
Rationale for the Change: Intel Currently has multiple Entitlement management tools, and they can consolidate them all and use SAP EMS instead, leading to reduction in overall TCO.

Change Title*: Integrations with Carriers through standard providers Vs the current OpenText has caused extra development.
Change Description:
Intel's carriers post the in-execution/transit events to Intel via GXS/OpenText. GXS/OpenText forwards the events to Intel's SAP EM system in the as-is scenario. In future state, the events will be received in SAP GTT from GXS/OpenText. Intel wants to integrate SAP GTT with GXS/OpenText which will require a major change on GXS/OpenText side to consume the standard OData Services provided by SAP GTT. IT is a huge development effort to consume these services by GTT into GXS/OpenText. Since Intel is bringing their own vendor to consume GTT services instead of preferred vendors, the integration has to be setup from scartch instead of using a pre-set integration with our preferred partners.

We suggest that the integrations with carriers should be done through standard preferred providers.
Rationale for the Change: SAP GTT also has preffered visibility providers onboarded on the network which can be levereged to get the visibility into the shipments from the carriers. The visibility providers internally connect to the carriers to get the events into SAP GTT. Following this approach will require much less effort from an onboarding perspective.

Change Title*: Logic update for Functional Area derivation in FPRE0023 & FPRE0024
Change Description:
This is a 0$ CR. Functional Area derivation logic in FPRE0023 & FPRE0024 needs to be updated as mentioned below:
1. If the transaction from ECC has a functional area for the 5 & 6 series GL accounts (Field HKONT) - Keep source functional area unless in GL range 588000 – 588999 
2. If the transaction from ECC does not have a functional area or is a 4 or 9 series GL (9 Series exception GL 919111, 919112, or 919113 account) or 5 & 6 series GL accounts (Field HKONT) - map based on Target GL Account   
3. If the transaction from ECC has a functional area for GL (Field HKONT) range 588000- 588999 – map based on Target GL Account  
4. If the transaction from ECC has a functional area for GL account 919111 or 919112, or 919113 clear the functional area at target system CFIN and proceed  
5. If the transaction from ECC has a functional area COS for the 5 & 6 series GL accounts (unless in GL Range 588000 – 588999)- map by Target GL account for COGS and INVI, all else goes to OCOS.
Rationale for the Change: According to the business requirement, the existing functional area logic needs to be updated to be aligned with Group Reporting.

Change Title*: Ability to publish scenario as POR​
Change Description:
The Business needs the ability to run What-if scenarios in BY ESP , publish these scenarios/solves to IF-PDH, designate 1 of these as the POR  and push chosen scenario to downstream applications via PDH. This is required in case the Tuesday baseline solve is infeasible, or non-optimal. ​

In case the Tuesday baseline solve run produces infeasible or non-optimal results,  planners can run what-ifs to:​

Change Title*: Creation of Consolidated CMR
Change Description: Creation of Consolidated CMR
Rationale for the Change:
In Old design, When the Confirm button was clicked, the GR was posted automatically in the background, and then system would then evaluate discrepancy results and post a separate CMR document according to different scenarios:
a)No Discrepancy: GR was posting immediately but CMR generation occurred only when the discrepancy status at the subline level was marked as "Cleared for Credit."
b) Discrepancy Identified : GR was posting immediately but If discrepancies were not resolved through the Discrepancy Action Matrix rules, the subline item was marked as discrepant. Salesforce would then provide the discrepancy resolution, and auto CMR Generation would be generated automatically only when the Discrepancy Status was "Accept = Cleared for Credit" using the same API.
This old approach led to challenges in account reconciliation for the business.
To improve the reconciliation process during settlement, it was suggested to generate consolidated CMRs based on RMA number, MMID, unit price, original PO, and return PO.
In the new design, the process for posting the Goods Receipt (GR) document remains unchanged. However, the consolidated CMR document will now be posted through a separate batch job, which groups subline records based on RMA number, MMID, subline status “Cleared for Credit,” unit price, original PO and Return PO.
When the Confirm button is clicked, the GR will be posted automatically in the background and then new batch job program will only consider sublines with the status “Cleared for Credit.” It will then verify that the RMA number, material code (MMID), and unit price are identical across these sublines. If all these criteria match, the program will generate a single consolidated CMR document.
 
Note – If Original PO and Original Invoice Number is needed in CMR and CN then we need to use the custom routine.

Change Title*: R2 Payer Master Data Conversion
Change Description:
Data Conversion: Replicate IF LLPs with new number/account in MDG/IF S/4
Changes needed:
1) For the LLPs, create new LLP with new # in new MDG under both 101/AM21 & 798/SG01.
2) Delink the old LLP from existing Sold To under IP LE/sales area and link the new LLP to the existing Sold To under IF LE/sales area.
Rationale for the Change:
Payers act as subledgers, which is why we need to keep IP related payers separate from IF payers to keep financial postings on the subledgers separate. This is common Intel practice for any large M&A activities where we separate business by LE.
By keeping financial postings on separate subledgers, we avoid multiple financial reconciliation issues such as: cross-company postings, misdirected cash postings, etc.
Type of Change [For Program Releases - R: 4. Change to Existing RICEFW / ECA/ Build Object
Notes from Change Ingestion:

Change Title*: Additional Finance Validations for GL, Profit Center & Cost Center
Change Description:
Additional MDG validations.

1. Duplicate name check: My team logged a defect but she was told that the duplicate name validation is only checked against backend table, not staging table. In legacy MDG, the validation is performed against staging table. Let’s say 2 GLs or 2 cost centers are created using the same name in 2 separate CRs and are both pending for approval, if the validation is only done against backend table, we will not know there is a duplicate until one is approved, right? I think it makes more sense to validate against staging table to make sure nodes with pending CRs are taken into consideration.

2. Profit center is not a required field in cost center master data, can we make it a required field? We have it as required field in cost center user story and it is considered a fit, thus, we didn’t call it out as a custom validation. But it seems profit center can be left blank in our testing.

Change Title*: Plan for delinquent RDD
Change Description:
We need the ability to plan and consume current allocations  for orders that have a request date within lead time and start these wafers immediately if allocations exist
Technical solution would be to push delinquent RDDs to CW in PDH prior to solve (only for new orders SO with no CDD), provide original RDD in BY for reference, and netting logic for orders will need to be extended to LT+ x weeks and will be different for each process
Rationale for the Change:
OTC has confirmed that customers can place orders with RDD in the future (e.g. tomorrow) and that RDD may be in the past when it reached BY, once hold is released. RDD cannot be changed in S4 due to customer restriction
Technical team confirmed that any orders that show up in BY with RDD prior to CW cannot not be planned as they are considered delinquent. Thus RDD in BY needs to be updated to CW
RDD between CW and CW+LT-12  for LT >12 will also be treated as “order with no RTF” even though capacity commits might exist for starts in CW+1. Thus change to the netting horizon is needed.
Notes from Change Ingestion: o	This change will have no downstream impact and will be contained. Slated for SCP UAT2.

Next Steps: On hold due to Separation - Planned for 3/7, but not reviewed.
Change Title*: SIMS – Change front office ops from LE-745 to LE-750
Change Description:
In mid-2024, Intel decided to outsource the manufacture of Test Interface Units “TIU’s” and Testing Units “TU’s” to 3rd party contract manufacturers previously manufactured internally by the SIMS team in LE-745. Intel Global Trade (GTS) team assessed which Intel Malaysia entity would be most appropriate to conduct front office support for coordination of ordering TIU’s  / TU’s from 3rd party contract manufacturers (e.g., Jabil). Transactions include receiving intercompany orders for TIU’s/TU’s from various Intel Foundry entities that require them, and placing orders to 3rd party contract manufacturers. Upon completion of manufacture, transact the receipt of the TIU/TU into LE-750 and fulfill the intercompany PO to the Intel Foundry entity that ordered it. It was determined that LE-750 would be most appropriate – this  combines retained SIMS processes and employees into one Malaysian entity.

Change Title*: Change in logic for the fields in confirmed line item in schedule line level, KPI fields and add 2 more fields.
Change Description:
Change in Logic for Fields in Confirmed Line Items at the Schedule Line Level: Currently, the system confirms the sales order quantity regardless of stock availability and ATP interim block at the schedule line level, which is determined by schedule line category CN. For R2, we must change the logic so that if there is an ATP interim block at the schedule line level. Once the block is removed, the confirmed quantity and date will be accurately reflected. The fields requiring changes are: Confirmed Quantity, Confirmed Delivery Date (CDD), Confirmed Material Availability Date (CMAD), and Confirmed GI Date (CGID).

Additionally, due to the changes in the above fields, there is an impact on the Customer Order Response Time KPI, and the logic needs to be adjusted for the original confirmation entry date. Currently, this field is calculated as follows: if the CDD is generated at the time of creation, then the Sales Order Line Create Date is used; if there is a CDD update after the sales order creation, then the date is taken from the change log tables. Now, due to the above changes, we need to adjust this logic.

Furthermore, two additional fields need to be added to capture the ECC sales order numbers and sales order line numbers in the backlog and Customer Order Inquiry Report from S4 Hana.
Rationale for the Change:

Change Title*:
1. Update Order Confirmation Output to Include Only Modified Line Items.
2. Update the rule matrix to include the new validations for triggering suppressing the outputs
Change Description:
This change request aims to update the logic for generating order confirmation outputs so that only the line items to which changes have been made are included. Line items that remain untouched should not be included in the change confirmation outputs. It also aims to incorporate specific validations for triggering or suppressing the outputs in various scenarios. The validations will ensure that the order acknowledgement and confirmation/change outputs are generated accurately based on the status of the line items and sales orders.

Current Behavior: The order confirmation output currently includes all line items, regardless of whether changes have been made to them or not.
Proposed Change: Modify the order confirmation output to include only the line items that have been modified. Line items that are untouched should be excluded from the change confirmation outputs.
Rationale for the Change:
To ensure that the order confirmation outputs are concise and only reflect the changes made to the order, thereby providing clear and relevant information to the customer.
To ensure that the outputs are triggered or suppressed accurately based on the status of the line items and sales orders, providing clear and relevant information to the customer. This change will enhance the accuracy and relevance of the information provided in the outputs, reducing potential confusion and improving customer communication.
Type of Change [For Program Releases - R: 4. Change to Existing RICEFW / ECA/ Build Object

Change Title*: CF BApp - ILM FOAM: Addition of new scope, budget request and design extension due to PTP Tower need for S4 QM integration wtih ILM FOAM along with Master Data
Change Description: New Scope for ILM FOAM to enable time sensitive Quality Module Activation interacting with SAP S4 and MDG, using a system that users are familiar and effective. ILM FOAM is the POR system for materials excursion. Additional scope for ILM FOAM includes: S4 QM integration/interfaces + Enhancements +  Support MDG manual interface.   Schedule change: Need extension of design till end of April, will continue to target EOY Build completion. Budget Request: $99k to cover the new scope of work in ILM FOAM. B-Apps will be funding the increased cost through Q1 underspends hence no additional ask for OSC/CL
Rationale for the Change: S4 Quality Module allows Intel to segregate materials of concern and document the decision process for use. The Quality Module (QM) activation is use-case driven and cannot be done during Material Master setup.  If QM is active all the time, material would be inefficiently over contained. QM is activated by the signals from the business including quality, stability, and readiness. Inspection needs are derived from severity of excursions scenarios. ILM is the POR system to material excursions which triggers QM activation. MDG did not comprehend the dynamic nature of the QM Master Data. QM activation will be simplified with automated/integrated solution b/w ILM, MDG and S4.
Notes from Change Ingestion:
o	From a previous CR, 2 interfaces were introduced into the PTP S4 development.
	ILM FOAM team was not aware of the work required from their end.
	Therefore, this CR will be to extend design and obtain $99K in budget to begin the work.
o	The ongoing separation design might affect this work, and the team is capturing the impacts.
	The team will need to come up with a back up plan in case this CR does not go through.
o	AR: Add in statement that this will be the design going forward; no decisions are being re-opened - @Chaudhry, Lopa
o	AR: Add in details about the high-level timeline on main CR template (Slide 1) - @Chaudhry, Lopa

The manual process involves triggering notifications for MDG users to enter data manually, as the automation with MDG was not approved. The team is working on new interfaces for ILM foam, which require UI changes and data inspection plans.  
•	Concerns raised about the expanding scope of quality automation, but the team agreed to focus on essential capabilities without overextending.
•	Decision:  Approved

Change Title*: Build Ahead Limits
Change Description: Since the demand prioritization scheme has a long span, there is a need to bake in Solver objectives that will limit the build ahead for the demand. At the same time, there is also a need to fine tune the build late parameter. The build late parameter is a configuration available in BY.
Rationale for the Change:
Currently, the Solver builds ahead for the demand that may be far out in future if the JIT capacity is constrained and there is some capacity to spare ahead of time. In this case, the demand will be met late. In some cases, the very far out demand is not known with high level of confidence so starting the wafers for that demand may result in excess inventory if the demand/forecast does not materialize. So users need a way to control how early the wafers can be started for future demand. ​

Similarly, if the current demand cannot be met within capacity constraints the demand is scheduled for future. If the demand is committed far out in future, there is a possibility of order cancellation and wafer started for that demand may result in excess inventory. So there is a need to control the build late parameter also.
Type of Change [For Program Releases - R: 4. Change to Existing RICEFW / ECA/ Build Object
Notes from Change Ingestion: Will require further approval from Core Leadership; o	AR: Quantify the “medium-high” effort and assess resource availability to perform the work - @Arciniaga, Michael P

Change Title*: IAO R2 BY design –Priority routing, split ratio for bump sort​
Change Description: Current requirement is for planning to be able to select the planned sites and routing for products to enable business continuity, efficient use of available labor resources, WLA requirements for co-location on tray line, customer driven requests, and cost minimization. Current BY capabilities will manage TPT minimization (at weekly level) and will only use current sites defined on the BOM to create production methods.
Rationale for the Change:
Currently sites that are loaded in BOM are not always qualified to run revenue material, and are in the BOM for other downstream systems (e.g., Prospal needs D1C, site needs to be on the BOM to extend material and start qualification on site). This can cause BY to plan wafers on non valid sites​

As TPTs are rounded to the weekly level, BY can plan to send wafers from OR to NM instead of keeping wafers in OR even though that would be the preferred route​

Colocation strategy in sort for BE and ATM capacity. Changes monthly​

BY transportation schedules are currently not synced with the execution schedule in SPAN. Having least impact on manufacturing was a key tenant for R2​

Not enough overall demand can push one site to be 100% loaded while the other to have less than optimal loading

Change Title*: Implementation of custom fields to capture and retain the original requested quantity
Change Description:
To enhance the tracking and comparison of requested quantities in the KPI report by capturing original values in custom fields.
1. The original requested quantity will be captured in a custom field upon the customer's initial submission.
2. If the customer revises the Purchase Order (PO), the standard requested quantity. The custom field will retain the original values, allowing for a comparison between the original and current requested quantity in the KPI report.
Only the CBA has access to manually update the original requested quantity in the custom field.
Rationale for the Change: The primary purpose of this change is to improve the accuracy and reliability of tracking requested and confirmed quantities in KPI reports. Change Title*: Sales Region -new custom object in MDG
Change Description: OTC-IP is requesting the creation of Sales Region as a custom governed object in MDG to support SMG & OTC-IP requirements to group Sales Organizations.
Rationale for the Change:
Today we have 4 different Geo Location Hierarchy Types – EN (enterprise), SM (Sales & Marketing), SMA (Sales & Marketing Analytics) and AC (Accounting).  As part of IAO, we are moving toward an industry standard single Global Geo Location Hierarchy (GL) with 3 Geo’s (APAC, AMER, EMEA).

It was initially proposed to break out China and India as independent sub geos in the IAO Global Geo Hierarchy to support the strategic importance and increased business focus on those regions specifically within SMG & OTC-IP 

After analysis, it was determined that the Geo Location Hierarchy has been used by SMG to model their Sales Regions to support their business operations versus a true Geo Hierarchy.  Sales Orgs were then associate to those SM “Geo’s”. 

The proposal is to create a Newly Governed object called Sales Region and to associate Sales Orgs to those Sales Regions.

It is recommended that the updates to the GL Hierarchy to include China and India as Sub-regions be reverted as the new Sales Region will support the reason for the previous recommendation.

Sales Region/Org Association and GL Geo Hierarchy will be two separate dimensions that can be used to roll up and filter transactional data as ALL customers have a Sales Org (Sales Region/Org Association) and a Country (Geo Hierarchy).

Sales Region will be separately governed by SMG Governing Body.
Comments for Rejection/Deferral: This CR is less than 40 hours of work, and a non-ERP RICEFW will be created and developed by the Intel team.

Change Title*: Interface from FSCO for lot level material staging by shift
Change Description:
FSCO publishes to S4 the list of lots required at production sites by dates/time which needs to be captured in a custom table in S4. This will be an interface to load the data published from FSCO into the custom table. ​

This table will be used to issue out lots either through a 313 movement if the warehouse location is same as production plant or through STO if warehouse is not the same as the production plant. This is part of the approved enhancement object FTSE0507.
Rationale for the Change: Without this interface there is no information available in S4 to determine when the lots are required at the production sites which would lead to delays and disoriented manufacturing process.
Notes from Change Ingestion:
o	All task force team meetings have been completed to work through manufacturing integration.

o	AR: Include summary slide of the work that has been going on – Rudra

•	There is no data or tech team impact as of now. Most of it is just from the FSCO and PDH Boundary Apps.
Notes from Core Leadership Review [Stage:
•	The recent changes to the ERP system are driven by new requirements identified by the manufacturing task force. These changes aim to optimize inventory management and logistics processes, particularly concerning the movement and scheduling of wafers and dies, as well as site-to-site transportation.
•	The process will now be driven by production scheduling at a more granular level, such as daily or shift-based requirements. This change requires:
o	An interface from FSCO to determine required lots shift by shift.
o	Storage of this data in a custom table.
o	Site-to-Site Transportation
•	New Plan: An algorithm in the Planning Data Hub (PDH) will assign shipments, necessitating:
o	A new interface from PDH to S4.
o	Shipping and STOs
•	S4 Role: S4 will act as a data provider in a hub-and-spoke model, but it also needs to execute shipping against STRs.
•	Instead of bulk STOs, lot-level STOs will be created when a lot is ready to ship, allowing logistics processes to create outbound deliveries.

Change Title*: Unable to generate DLCP Calendar
Change Description:
Current design generates BY DLCP UPI/BOM for items linked to SPEED FG items with SPAN die seg value > “0” . Change Title*: Chengdu Ebook Serial Number Changes
Change Description: Aligning system and business process with the information required by China Customs.
Rationale for the Change:
Clean-up misunderstanding between Operations team and the design team on what is required by China Customs. There are two types of data with similar names. Ebook Serial number refers to the company/operator who is shipping/receiving within the country. This can be considered a company Id for Customs filing purposes and is a fixed number at the plant level. Ebook number refers to the unique number assigned to a product within the plant. This is used to track goods and assess tariffs and taxes due.
​

Today, the export documentation only has the “Ebook Serial Number” which is incorrect.

This requirement will be covered under OTCE1199. As aligned for this object as well we have 2 tables for which data is to be converted but since business also has to ensure that data is cleansed in ECC and the nature of table in not complex. Hence, the object is no longer required.

Change Title*: Change Order to ERP IT & Business Controls Narratives
Change Description: This change request proposes incorporating IT & Business controls narrative into the project scope to provide a more comprehensive understanding of the organization's IT systems and business processes embedded within IT systems and each business process, which includes system description, risks, controls, roles and responsibilities, respectively. Initially, this element was not included in the project scope; however, as we finalize the design and testing phases, we seek approval to proceed with this update to align with the revised requirements
Rationale for the Change: The rationale for including IT and business narratives in the project scope, particularly for SOX/Audit compliance, is to ensure thorough documentation and integration of controls that align business objectives with IT capabilities, thereby enhancing compliance, risk management, and operational efficiency.
Notes from Change Ingestion:
- This change is for Deloitte to document the controls and process narratives for each process. Each E2E process is listed out with all of the roles and security controls that are involved.
- There are some existing roles narratives, but the security team will enhance it with best practices and ensure consistency across as there are a lot of roles being added for R1/R2. 
- The control narrative is owned currently by the control owner, and there are many. So, there a slight differences.
- High-level security roles are different from the controls narrative. It is the entire lifecycle of a role to be executed, including post-go-live. If a role needs to be executed after everything is live, then defined process needs to be executed to make sure that everything passes.
- Original SOW was only scoped to identify risks. As the team progressed, they realized that this is missing to fulfill external auditors who will be examining the new system in Q3. The security narratives are not linked to go-live.
- The team will need an answer in the next 2 weeks to complete the narratives on time, but it is not a showstopper.
- AR: Determine whether there is a trade-off to the current work in flight. - Yaz
- AR: Determine how much credit was given back from descoping DPP and understand how much the security narratives cost. - Yaz, Alpna, Anand
Notes from Core Leadership Review [Stage: Per our discussion last week, the estimate for the Security team’s change request (CR0680) to add Business Controls narrative and Non Production Security roles work to Deloitte scope, is $259K. Security team is requesting this be netted off against the already approved change request (CR0479) that was done to descope Data Privacy. That approved CR would have credited back $227K.

Change Title*: Material Hazmat Profile Management Capability
Change Description:
Request: Need to enable maintenance of Hazardous Material Profile to be used within the Factory Portal Application. The hazmat profile advises Factory and Warehouse workers on proper handling and shipping of hazardous inventory returns. ​

Recommendation: Build a new application to enable maintenance of hazardous material profile using UI5 technology stack. The data is created and accessed from the same system.​
Rationale for the Change: The design for maintenance of  hazmat profile for materials was not finalized during L3 Design Phase.  Options were explored but not decided. This CR is to review options considered and get approval for the proposed option.
Notes from Change Ingestion:
o	Need a profile to be generated and tagged to materials so that the hazardous material team will know how to handle it. It will come through the Factory Portal UI.
o	This is not part of the original design scope.
•	The original plan was to explore options in ECM, SAP EHS module, eCUDL, and custom REACT application. 
•	eCUDL said no because it is not their scope of work. High-volume use cases are taken care of by MBOM. It would be a re-write if they took on the work.
•	MBOM would have to be changed to a Tolerate Boundary App
o	The aligned option is a small footprint application.
Will need to go to CR Meeting
Notes from Core Leadership Review [Stage:
o	Required to enable maintenance of Hazardous Material Profile to be used within the Factory Portal Application. The hazmat profile advises factory and warehouse workers on proper handling and shipping of hazardous inventory returns. 
o	Existing WIINGS module is too deeply embedded to extract.
o	Alternatives (React app, EHS Center of Excellence, SAP EHS module) were evaluated and rejected due to complexity, cost, or misalignment with needs.
o	Recommended solution: Build a lightweight UI5 app in NF4.
o	Estimate: 260 hours (~$21K), to be funded by Intel Foundry vs. IAO.
o	Concerns Raised, Mike Lange and Todd Smith questioned the long-term strategy of building custom apps instead of leveraging SAP modules. Clean core violations were discussed but clarified as standard SAP customizations, not core modifications. Cari Shim supported the short-term tradeoff for cost and expediency.
o	Confirmed that only two modules remain: eDFIT and the hazardous material profile app.

Change Title*: IAO R2 BY-SPAN Schedule (FSCO FAB , BUMP , SORT WIP + BY Planned Order)
Change Description: IAO R2 BY-SPAN Schedule is not the combination of FSCO FAB , BUMP , SORT WIP + BY Planned Order
Rationale for the Change:
In the current design, the previous cycle BY Planned Orders roll into the frozen horizon and are not adjusted. However, in BY we are not using the previous planned orders as our statement of supply, we are using the FSCO Projections, so BY will adjust the forward-looking planned orders based on how much WIP is in the line. Whenever the WIP in line varies from the original planned order in either quantity or time, we get a misalignment when we stitch together the old planned orders within frozen and the new planned orders outside frozen to generate the POR schedule. 
This existing design does not meet the need for reliable supply schedules or demand schedules described above.
Impact to Schedule: Solution won't be ready until cycle 2 of UAT2
Notes from Change Ingestion:
o	The target is to complete all of the work in UAT2 at the start, but the second requirement won’t be ready until the end of UAT2.
•	Will impact some of the current R3 deliverables to complete this work, but it is relatively small. The team is hopeful to recover.
o	There is no viable manual workaround.
Notes from Core Leadership Review [Stage:
•	Proposal: Split the POR schedule into two:
o	Supply Schedule: Reflects expected output from Fab, Bump, and Sort.
o	Demand Schedule: Reflects what is needed to meet demand, back projected from sales orders.
•	Effort Estimates:
o	Supply schedule: ~1 week (low effort).
o	Demand schedule: ~5 weeks (higher effort, spans UAT2 Cycle 1).
•	Risks:
o	Span team resource reallocation may delay R3 deliverables.
o	Manual workarounds deemed too complex and error prone.
•	Decision: Proceed with changes despite risks, leveraging lighter Sprint 14–15 capacity.

Change Title*: Modeling PO Request Date to align with BY weekly buckets
Change Description:
Suppliers require that our order due dates align to a specific day of the week, rather than be scattered throughout the week.
This request is to adjust the due date when creating the orders in SAP ECC, aligning to the specified day of the week.
The targeted day of the week must fall within the same Blue Yonder weekly bucket.  This change should only apply to PDH, no change required in BY and no impact to PRF.
Current PDH logic edits the RDD for Internal (-1D) and External (-2D).  Change request is to change logic for Internal (+6D) and External (+5D).

Additionally, scheduled receipts should be adjusted to the beginning of the Blue Yonder weekly bucket (on Sunday).
Rationale for the Change: Prior requirement was to adjust due date by going backwards to the defined day of the week. However, this crossed BY time buckets causing a disconnect between the bucket in which the order was planned and the time bucket in which the order is placed.
Type of Change [For Program Releases - R: 4. Change to Existing RICEFW / ECA/ Build Object
Notes from Change Ingestion:
o	Change the PO request date in PDH to align better what both external and internal customers expect
•	This is a change in logic to push it to the +6 or +5, and use the next Friday/Saturday that the PO’s are created.

Change Title*: Reclaim Wafer material movement - I/b and O/B - Design needs
Change Description:
The Reclaim Wafer Working Group (X-Tower) has aligned on the need to initiate a CR to support the program’s progression and enable formal resource assignment based on the findings derived from the MES analysis. This CR is a critical step to ensure that we have the appropriate backing and resources required to address the identified issues effectively and move forward with solution implementation.

Additionally, during the recent trade discussions, Marianna raised concerns regarding the viability of incorporating ISM in the proposed "To-Be" state. She indicated that achieving this integration may not be feasible given the current system and process constraints.

We recommend moving forward with the CR creation to formally capture the required changes and to align stakeholders on next steps -  FTS RAID 01395 created.
Rationale for the Change: The CR is driven by a design miss from Deloitte in the end-to-end process mapping where reclaim wafer handling is not included, which has resulted in critical gaps that now require remediation through formal change control.
Impact to Schedule: E2E design ( process impact)  of reclaim wafer handling needs to be completed and be incorporated into the existing build to support R3 IAO timelines.
Notes from Change Ingestion:
o	There is a process for reclaim wafers, and it is handled through ISM. However, the team found out that it cannot continue in that app.
o	This request is to ask for E2E team involvement to include in the To-Be design and for a path-finding effort to map wafers out to suppliers.
•	The team has a conceptual design, and they have done a high-level estimate.
Notes from Core Leadership Review [Stage:
•	The reclaim wafers process must be redesigned due to compliance issues with ISM, which is no longer viable.
•	A new process flow has been designed. The key challenge is how to induct reclaim wafer inventory into S4.
•	Option 1: Integration with SMH (original recommendation) is not feasible within the current timeline.
•	Option 2: A custom Fiori wrapper transaction around MEGO is proposed to simplify user interaction and ensure compliance.
•	Option 3: Use standard MEGO directly, but this poses risk due to complexity and potential for user error.
•	The wrapper transaction will not be ready for ITC1 but can be tested in ITC2. MEGO will be used as a fallback for ITC1.
o	The process is low risk because:
•	It affects a small portion of the overall flow.
•	A known manual workaround (MEGO) exists.
•	Concerns were raised about volume (up to 2,000 transactions/week), which may strain manual processing.
•	There are compliance-related data requirements (e.g., country of origin, diffusion) that must be addressed in the wrapper.
•	The CR was not approved in this meeting. A follow-up review is required to finalize design details and validate assumptions.
•	AR: Todd Smith and Eugene Celis: Finalize wrapper transaction design. Address compliance fields, batch creation and usability.
•	AR: Todd Smith: schedule a review with Roman Vorel. Clarify RICEF scope, conflicts and finalize proposal. 
•	AR:  Todd Smith bring CR-0811 back WW32.5. Include one-pager justification for late ITC2 entry.

•	Followed up on a previously rejected CR regarding reclaim wafer outbound handling.
•	The team proposed a custom GUI wrapper application with a Fiori tile to reduce complexity and effort compared to the original high-complexity Fiori UI approach. 
•	This medium-risk CR is scheduled for ITC2 and involves coordination across multiple teams including FTC, PTP, LE, and EWM. 
o	Master data and PTP teams will each spend 32 hours setting up and mapping six generic MM IDs for the process.
o	LE, EWM, and TM scope for inventory movement and outbound processes is covered in CR1035. 
•	The solution aims to streamline the process of returning inventory from the factory floor to suppliers 
•	Decision: Approved

Change Title*: Extend ASP forecast (Finance) horizon and ASP Forecast determination logic
Change Description:
1. POR ASP forecasted quarterly by Finance using Revenue (SAP IBP) solution. This forecast currently done for ~4-5Qs (DPOR goes 5 Qs). However, Consensus Demand covers 10Qs.  This gap leads to partial dollarization of the demand.
2. Missing ASPs for products introduced between Quarterly POR ASP forecast process. Leads to understatement of dollarized demand. We can take an algorithmic approach to derive ASPs for such products 
3. Store/report ASPs derived algorithmically. Original ASPs received from Finance are already preserved.
Rationale for the Change:
- Dollarized demand values are with gaps (horizon as well as missing products)
- Agreement is in place with Finance to forecast the entire horizon, but systemic changes are needed to extract the data for entire horizon
- Algorithmic approach is good enough for missing products. We need to preserve such computed ASPs for traceability purposes
Type of Change [For Program Releases - R: 4. Change to Existing RICEFW / ECA/ Build Object
Notes from Change Ingestion:
o	If this is not done, then the dollarized demand values will be lower than normal.
o	The 10Q ASP is a must-have part of the request, and the team is planning to deliver in UAT2.
•	The other requests will be delivered afterwards in WW42.
•	The change is contained in IP.

Change Title*: Custom MDG report logic change to get rid of dependency on next edition
Change Description:
Custom MDG report logic needs to be change to get rid of dependency on next edition given Next edition is not maintained in MDG based on new business process.

Without the change, the report will not return any data.  The reports are leveraged by IT to enable data load to legacy MDG.
Rationale for the Change: Custom MDG reports compare CR creation date with the valid from date of the edition in the prompt as well as the valid from date of the next edition in the edition table.  However, next edition is not maintained in the new business process to prevent users from misusing the wrong edition to create hierarch related changes, thus, the logic is broken and no data is returned.   This issue was not applicable in UAT testing because next edition was maintained in UAT.  It’s in production that we decided to not maintain next edition to avoid the version issues from UAT results.  We need to change the report logic to make report work.  Instead of using next edition to derive the end date, the report should look for DAY7 edition of the same month.  If DAY7 edition is not available either, just take today’s date.
Type of Change [For Program Releases - R: 4. Change to Existing RICEFW / ECA/ Build Object
Notes from Change Ingestion: o	This request is a small logic change to see both future and current editions. The team can only see the current edition.

Change Title*: Requesting for additional overpack functionality – Label change & HU Close
Change Description: Requesting for additional overpack functionality – Label change & HU Close
Rationale for the Change:
In the WIP process when the goods movement happens from one factory to another, warehouse will be printing the overpack label . In R3, when dealing with multiple deliveries packed in a single overpack HU, the over pack label should be printing DN reference as XXXX to avoid confusion during order fulfilment. Printing of the overpack label should be enabled from the HU Close step for efficiency.​

Additionally, while the HU’s of multiple DNs are manually overpacked which are to be delivered in single TU, then the overpacking should happen only for the HUs which are tagged to the deliveries assigned to the TU ie., HU’s related to other TU’s should not be allowed to be overpacked mistakenly.
Notes from Change Ingestion:
o	This CR is trying to replicate the current process and initiate cost savings.
	We need enhancements and interfacing to make it happen.
o	All work will be contained in SAP.
Notes from Core Leadership Review [Stage:
•	Background & Proposal: Rodolfo explained the current overpacking process is using B-apps. Overpacking involves consolidating multiple delivery notes into one large box, simplifying the shipping process.  Path of least resistance involves shipping smaller boxes individually, which may lead to increased freight costs and packaging material usage.  The redesign aims to mimic the current process in SAP EWM, focusing on label redesign and handling unit closing.  If approved Rodolfo to lead the implementation of the overpacking process in SAP EWM, including label redesign and handling unit closing.  
•	Cost Implications: Discussed the cost savings associated with the overpacking process, estimating a difference of around $200K if the process is implemented correctly.  Overpacking provides substantial savings, especially for R3 and Ronler Acres and Aloha sites.  Increased freight costs and minimum charges are considerations if smaller boxes are shipped individually.  Cost Analysis Team to conduct a detailed cost analysis considering increased freight costs and minimum charges for shipping smaller boxes.  
•	Technical and Functional Assessment: Approximately 232 hours are estimated for label redesign, technical and functional unit tests, and integration with the current R2 process. The customization is included in the 232 hours, with no additional RICEFWs expected.  Logistics and tech teams have reviewed the redesign and confirmed capacity to handle it without impacting other work.  There are no master data changes or B-Apps impacts are expected. The team has confirmed capacity to handle the redesign without impacting other workstreams.  
•	Decision: Approved to proceed with the overpacking process redesign in SAP EWM, focusing on label redesign and handling unit closing.

Change Title*: Plan Sort Out Wafer Purchases
Change Description:
In UAT1 it was discovered that both OneMPS and BY IBP are planning a subset of products. These are unique in that they do not have a fab part on the SPEED BOM and appear as Sort only products (Examples: Carlsville, Linkville, Gothic Bridge). In today’s environment, these would be treated as OSAT purchases and the OneMPS team reacted accordingly.
Subsequently, the determination was made that these are initial wafer purchases that belong in BY IBP.
Change is to remove these from planning in OneMPS which requires changes to both SPAN and OneMPS.
Rationale for the Change: Ensure that only one planning system is generating requirements for this set of products.
Impact to Schedule:
There won’t be availability for the analysis of any other R2 CR’s
Thorough testing will be required to ensure no impact to production release
No capacity to support R3 microplanning sessions
Type of Change [For Program Releases - R: 4. Change to Existing RICEFW / ECA/ Build Object
Notes from Change Ingestion:
o	These wafer purchases need to be removed from planning in OneMPS as it was not considered for SPAN and OneMPS.
•	BY is sending out discreet PO’s, and OneMPS Is using scheduline lines. Therefore, if it is not removed, BY will fail to plan wafers and products properly.
o	The team is pulling in additional resources to support this. Otherwise, both BY and OneMPS will do the planning.
•	It will be delivered in WW29, and there will be parallel testing done to mitigate the risk.
•	On SPAN side, we are pulling some non-IAO resources. As a tradeoff, if there are changes in the upstream, then the team will not be able to support it. No capacity to support R3 microplanning sessions.

Change Title*: Processing Stage attribute data to project the correct BUMP inventory
Change Description: For processing Stage attribute data to project the correct BUMP inventory due to CIBR error of capturing it as Sort inventory
Rationale for the Change: Without this change there are ~2 million die that are miscategorized in our inventory capture
Impact to Schedule: TBD - solution is still being worked
Type of Change [For Program Releases - R: 4. Change to Existing RICEFW / ECA/ Build Object
Notes from Change Ingestion: Reviewed in CL Meeting and added directly to the CR Meeting on Friday.
Notes from Core Leadership Review [Stage:
•	Problem: The CIBR system prematurely overrides the shop floor product name from the bump operation to the sort operation due to shared operations between the two. This misclassification causes inventory to appear under the wrong product, leading to planning and capacity misalignments.  A concern was raised regarding the impact on the SMART System, John acknowledged but noted that the exact impact needs further verification. 
•	Impact: Inventory appears as if it has passed through sort when it is still at bump, affecting sort TPT and capacity planning.
•	Proposed Solution: Leverage a new lot attribute from factory automation that indicates the planning stage. This attribute will be used to traverse the BOM and correctly associate the inventory with the appropriate UPI.
•	Timeline:  SPAN team, 6 weeks expected to be ready by WW32.  ECA team, 8 weeks expected to be ready by WW33. Teams have already begun work on the implementation.
Risk: The changes lands late in the SCP UAT2 cycle, POR end date of WW34 providing minimal time for testing.
•	Decision:  Approved. 
o	Deployment timing and Go Live Risk will be handled separately from the CR approval.
•	AR: John Heiney to confirm whether SMART requires a code change, as it was flagged as a potential consumer of CIBR data.
•	AR: John Heiney, Rebecca Verette and Sunil Narayanan to assess B-App impact and provide a holistic view.
•	AR: Rebecca Verette and John Heiney to coordinate and close out of data quality concerns.

Next Steps: Rick Antonellis and team to provide comprehensive risk assessment and Go Live recommendation for R2 SCP.

Change Title*: Retain CATTS/TCA (traceability) and ECD (functionality) for IP
Change Description:
Ensure continued access to CATTS/TCA and ECD for the Intel Products QREs with the responsibility to trace and contain lots in the IP Warehouse.​

Request an exception to allow the CATTS/TCA system to pull data from both IF ECA and IP ECA and allow both IF and IP users to access the CATTS/TCA and ECD systems.  ECD system must be able to block/contain inventory in both IF S/4 and IP S/4
Rationale for the Change:
Intel Products (IP) faces critical challenges in managing traceability and containment across its supply chain, especially as it transitions under IDM 2.0. The current Plan of Record (POR) does not support scalable, automated traceability, and lot holds/disposition in the warehouse essential due to the complexity of operations involving lot splits, merges, and unit-level tracking.​

CATTS/TCA and ECD are currently used to trace and contain all IF and IP inventory, including inventory from external foundry and OSAT for IP.  IP does not have an alternative method to trace IP owned inventory​
Impact to Schedule: Potential impact depending on how much effort is required from CATTS/TCA team to apply security.

Change Title*: Auto extension of C & C Roles AND CFIN replication issue for existing Payers from MDG
Change Description:
Changes needed:
1) Add automated BP role extension to new Payers as they move from MDG to CFIN.
Implement BADI PARTNER_UPDATE in CFIN to automatically create Credit & Collection roles when a payer is created having specific account group
2) When Payer changes are replicated from MDG to CFIN, where Payer is already having Credit & Collection Roles, the Changes should be synced with CFIN and It should NOT give Sync error - "Unable to delete Credit & Collection roles"
Rationale for the Change:
1) When a Payer is created in MDG and lands in S4/CFIN, the below roles should be extended automatically:
Credit Management
Collections Management

Rationale:
If payer role extension is manual, there is a risk that customer set-up will not be completed accurately, leading to overall delay in downstream processes (invoicing customers, credit checks, collections process)
Manual payer set-up will be a new process. As a result, additional steps and time required to prevent or mitigate incorrect role extension
This is a current OOB functionality available; loss of functionality in ECC today

2) When Payer changes are replicated from MDG to CFIN, where Payer is already having Credit & Collection Roles, the Changes should be synced with CFIN and It should NOT give Sync error - "Unable to delete Credit & Collection roles"

Rationale:
MDG system is always in Sync with CFIN system. However, as per Intel Design, Credit & Collections roles will be ONLY assigned in CFIN and not MDG. Hence, there is a Sync issue which happens whenever Payer is replicated from MDG to CFIN. 
Hence, In order to sync both the Systems, Whenever Changes are migrated from MDG to CFIN for already existing Payers, the C & C roles should be copied to Replication framework for incoming MDG data, so that the Incoming data from MDG & CFIN system are in Sync
Type of Change [For Program Releases - R: 4. Change to Existing RICEFW / ECA/ Build Object

Change Title*: IAO R2 – Unexpected Design Change Requires Boundary Applications Change (POWER/XETA)- Showstopper
Change Description:
The recent design change initiated by R2 Architecture Team has introduced a new signal called "Demand Schedule." 
Demand signal will replace the existing POR schedule used by Manufacturing and Planning as the weekly target. POWER and XETA systems need to adjust their sources to pull Demand Schedules from SPAN. 
POWER is the POR tool used to monitor current week actual PAS by stage and facility, including the OTD indicator.
XETA remains the scheduling and production control system for the manufacturing floor in R2 as POR system in D1 and F11x, while other facilities transition to FSCO.
With the introduction of the Demand Schedule, both POWER and XETA systems must switch to consume this signal as the schedule target. 

*FSCO has development in process to get the demand signal from SPAN.
Rationale for the Change:
Failure to implement these changes will result in factories lacking a POR schedule to meet floor targets and fulfill customer commitments. 
The absence of the POWER system in R2 means there will be no capability to track the current week's customer commit PAS or monitor the OTD indicator. 
Change needs to be implemented before UAT2 to be able to test POWER and XETA within R2 architecture.
Impact to Schedule: The CR is a showstopper for R2 from MFG and Planning perspective.
Notes from Change Ingestion:
o	CR0746 didn’t fully encompass the E2E impact. So, this CR will expand upon it as it is critical for R2.
o	Everything is the same format. This request is to adjust the logic to point to different data.
o	The POWER and XETA teams will be impacted.
o	AR: Include this risk to the list of risks impacting R3 – Sunil N.
Notes from Core Leadership Review [Stage:
•	Minor change is needed to switch from the POR supply schedule to the demand schedule for Power and Zeta applications.
•	The change is technically simple—just a filter switch—but requires a minor update on the Power side.
•	Confirmed the change intersects with UAT2 which adds a small risk, and has low impact on R3 due to internal adjustments.
•	Delivery is expected by WW30, following the availability of the Span solution in WW28.4.
•	Estimated effort: 50–60 hours total, with 20–30 hours for build.

Change Title*: CR for IDD E2E requirement gathering & design review​
Change Description:
The CR is for requirement gathering & review of design solution for the below user story :​

Intel is reevaluating the Intel Demo Depot (IDD) fulfillment process following the removal of the planned ISM integration from the IAO scope. ​

The ISM interface was deprioritized due to resource constraints, shifting business priorities, and minimal impact to business value.​

To ensure continuity and compliance, a replacement process is needed. A SO/PO model using a generic IPN has been proposed but will need overall E2E coordination for completion.
Rationale for the Change: The ISM interface was deprioritized due to resource constraints, shifting business priorities, and minimal impact to business value.
Notes from Change Ingestion:
o	This is a CR to understand the process for IDD (demo depot) and propose alternatives
•	This request is to do the requirements gathering and propose design
o	AR: Update slide to ensure all impacted teams are captured and what roles are required to complete the analysis
o	AR: Include when the analysis will be complete
Notes from Core Leadership Review [Stage:
Request:  Requirement gathering and review of design solution for the below user story :
•	Intel is reevaluating the Intel Demo Depot (IDD) fulfillment process following the removal of the planned ISM integration from the IAO scope. 
•	The ISM interface was deprioritized due to resource constraints, shifting business priorities, and minimal impact to business value.
•	To ensure continuity and compliance, a replacement process is needed.  The new SAP Fiori App that replaces ISM is NSO, No Stock Orders.
•	A SO/PO model using a generic IPN has been proposed but will need overall E2E coordination for completion.  
•	Effort: Functional Estimate is 80 hours for the initial assessment E2E, OM, Logistics, PTP. Team will perform assessment in less time than 80 hours.
o	FPR, Master Data, B-Apps representatives will only be included in the assessment if SO/PO model isn't viable.
•	Decision: Approved to assess the requirements gathering and review of design solution for Intel Demo Depot with E2E, OM, Logistics, PTP and IDD representatives.

•	Presented a follow-up on a previous change request (CR) regarding Intel Demo Depot (IDD) shipping processes. The team was asked to explore Option 1, which treats IDD as outside IAO, allowing them to build and ship products using the new Intel shipping memo capability.
•	During investigation, it was found that inappropriate items (e.g., wafers, finished goods) were being shipped under the Demo Depot category, raising concerns from the trade group.
•	The proposed solution includes:
o	Proceeding with Option 1.
o	Creating non-valuated, non-inventory materials for IDD.
o	Consulting with Global Trade for compliance.
o	Introducing a new item class specific to IDD to restrict access via security controls.
o	Using the NSO (Non-Stock Shipping Order) Fiori app with added security layers.
•	The goal is to prevent unauthorized use of shipping categories and enforce proper trade compliance through process controls and security restrictions.
•	The design is considered closed, and the next step is to implement the solution through new CRs.
•	Next steps:
o	Finalize Security Design: Define and implement security controls to restrict access to the new item class for authorized IDD personnel only.
o	Coordinate with Global Trade: Ensure IDD consults with Global Trade for each shipment to maintain compliance.
o	Develop New CRs: Submit new change requests to implement the proposed item class and security enhancements.
o	Assign Ownership: Identify and assign long-term ownership for maintaining the new item class and associated processes.
•	AR - Jo Guedes - Confirm Deloitte’s resourcing and timing for Demo Depot (NSO) security changes.
•	Decision: Intel Demo Depot will proceed with option one and implement the solution through new CRs.

•	The process starts with the supply plan generating overall substrate demand, which is then allocated to suppliers using MAPPS logic. MAPPS sends a consolidated Manufacturing Order (MO) request to the substrate supplier, aggregating both IP and IF requirements to present a unified Intel demand. Upon receiving responses from the substrate supplier, Intel disaggregates the information for internal IP and IF tracking. This approach is intended to prevent suppliers from distinguishing between IP and IF orders, supporting strategic alignment and volume management. 
•	The change request to support this process involves ECA work to enable aggregation and disaggregation, with a 22-week estimated effort and no impact to POR R3 timelines. The rationale is to appear as a single Intel entity to suppliers, simplifying interactions and avoiding supplier-driven prioritization between IP and IF. 
•	Decision:  The decision is to proceed with the change request enabling MAPS to send consolidated MO requests and aggregate/disaggregate substrate demand, ensuring suppliers see a unified Intel order and supporting strategic alignment, with no impact to POR R3 timelines.

•	Deep Trench Capacitor (DTC) is a specialized, high-cost component etched into silicon substrates, required at the start of substrate manufacturing for advanced packaging solutions. The new business model requires Intel to procure and consign DTCs to substrate suppliers due to their criticality and cost, differing from conventional Land Side Capacitors (LSC). The process involves CMP (Consolidation Materials Planning) generating purchase requests for DTCs, which are then sent to ECC (and in the future, S4). 
•	The change request is to enable this consignment and planning process for IP in CMP, with a new planning area in SAP IBP, ensuring DTC supply aligns with substrate manufacturing orders. The solution is designed to be ready for ITC one, with no impact to critical timelines, and is distinct from the IF process, which is being designed separately. The rationale for this approach is the critical nature and long lead time of DTCs, and the need for Intel to manage supply and ownership directly. 
•	Decision: The decision is to approve the change request to implement DTC consignment and planning for IP in CMP, using a new planning area in SAP IBP, with the solution targeted for ITC one and no impact to critical timelines.

Change Title*: Inclusion of expedite Fee in Current RAR Design (Fulfillment Based on POD of Hardware)
Change Description:
Currently, for hardware orders under Incoterm D with a revenue trigger based on Proof of Delivery (POD), both IP and IF are managed in RAR, while software and services are handled through Manual Fulfilment in RAR for IP only. The OTC team now plans to introduce a new line item in the sales order called "Expedite Fees." This fee will apply when customers request faster delivery of hardware within the same sales order.

To support this change:

A new POB (Performance Obligation) for Expedite Fees will need to be created, along with all necessary configurations in RAR for both IP and IF.
The fulfilment of Expedite Fees will be tied to the POD of the associated hardware item. This will require updates to the logic in the FPRE0760_IP and FPRE0760_IF objects, so that the TM module can trigger the fulfilment based on the hardware’s POD.
Rationale for the Change: The business requires that revenue for hardware items is recognized or deferred based on when the Proof of Delivery (POD) occurs. Since the Expedite Fees are directly related to delivering that hardware faster, it makes sense to recognize or defer the revenue for these fees in the same way as the hardware itself. This ensures that both the hardware and the associated expedite fees are treated consistently from a revenue accounting perspective.
Type of Change [For Program Releases - R: 4. Change to Existing RICEFW / ECA/ Build Object

Change Title*: Utility Program for Resolving AIF Errors Linked to Reference Documents/Invoices from Previous Years
Change Description: Currently, accounting documents or invoices that reference documents posted in previous years (FY24 or earlier) encounter posting failures in Central Finance (CFIN) and become stuck in the Application Interface Framework (AIF) due to errors related to these references. Change Title*: NEX BU Mount Morgan Memory Bailment Support
Change Description:
Pushed to R5.  The NEX business unit has won a couple of designs with Google to sell them the Mount Morgan IPU device. Approximate lifetime revenue is $1B.

•During mid-2024, Intel negotiated a bailment model for MMG 400 SoC memory.
•The bailment model meant a 3rd party agent would own memory on MMG 400 when assembled for Google, helping avoid a margin deterioration of ~10%.
•All plans and forecasts have assumed the bailment model, with memory being excluded from product ASP and cost for MMG 400 and MMG 800.

To meet Intel management’s product margin targets for the IPU, the business unit requests memory bailment. Google will not allow us to mark up the memory that is integrated into the IPU device. In this case, Google has already agreed to memory bailment. Depending on timeframes, NEX BU margins could degrade up to 10% if there is no memory bailment.

Notes from Core Leadership Review [Stage:
•	HB Loader, a tool used primarily in the backend assembly, test, and finish areas of Intel Foundry. Its main function is to translate product device references into factory-executable instructions for assembly and testing. The current system resides in SPAN, and there is a plan to migrate it into the Intel Foundry Planning Data Hub and integrate it with MARS to support factory teams exclusively.
•	Concerns were raised about the architectural implications of this move. The Planning Data Hub, originally designed as a clean, foundational planning data store, risks becoming overloaded with factory scheduling and preprocessing functions, potentially replicating the messy architecture of the legacy SPAN system. The split of scheduling functionality across different teams and systems (TD, SMDS, and Planning Data Hub) was highlighted as a challenge. It was noted that the initial idea to place this functionality inside production scheduling was rejected, leading to the current plan for IFPDH integration. The group agreed on the need to bring this topic to the IF Architecture Committee for review to ensure a long-term, clean solution rather than a quick fix.

•	Migration from SPAN to IF PDH is planned due to rejection of production scheduling ownership. Architectural concerns about overloading the Planning Data Hub were expressed.
•	Agreement to pursue architecture committee review before proceeding further.

Change Title*: Additional WRICEF to support Interface to deliver the MM, BOM, Vendor master, Batch and STO details

A single driver program will be implemented to send data—including Material Master, BOM, Vendor Master, Batch SLED, and STO details—to DMRA in intervals. An enhancement ID will be needed with this change request to enable scheduling the program as a background job.

Change Title*: Change in Putaway Strategy in MY01, CD01 Warehouse
Change Description: Currently, multiple batches of the same product are allocated to different bins during putaway. This leads to the same product being distributed across several storage locations, regardless of bin capacity. Under the new putaway strategy, multiple batches of the same product will be allocated to the same bin until the bin reaches its maximum capacity threshold. Only after a bin is full will subsequent batches be directed to additional bins. This change will be implemented across all AT sites, specifically MY01 and CD01
Rationale for the Change: Improved Space Utilization: Allocating multiple batches to the same bin maximizes bin capacity, reducing unused space and optimizing warehouse storage.

Change Title*: Time log for CFIN table to support C14 reporting
Change Description: Implement time dependent Profit Center mapping in S4 CFIN environment to support C14 reporting and Trial Balance reconciliation
Rationale for the Change:
Out of Box, Profit Center mapping in Central Finance (CFIN) does not support time dependency. Consequently, any updates to Profit Center mapping are applied immediately, and transactions replicated from ECC reflect the most recent mapping.​

This approach has several implications:​

Transactions occurring during overlapping periods are posted to the new Profit Center. ​

Historical reporting for external and segment reports using C14 (reorganization) is inaccurate due to the absence of time-dependent mapping. ​

Trial Balance reconciliation reports cannot be generated for historical periods. ​

FICO reconciliation reports (ECC to CFIN) are impacted.​
