Change Title*: API Issue
Change Description:
1. Enhancement to delete The standard tables ESH_SR_LTXT and ESH_SR_TXT_OBJ are utilized to replicate long texts from the SAP Script tables STXH and STXL. 

2. Enhancement is to create a Custom field in standard API in API_PURCHAEORDER_2, API_PURCHASECONTRACT_PROCESS_SRV VERSION 2 & API_PURCHASEREQUISITION_2 (OData v4) to capture Line item Amount in USD. Also need add new filed in API_PURCHAEORDER_2 for Incoterm Location ID in API
Rationale for the Change:
1. Enhancement to delete The standard tables ESH_SR_LTXT and ESH_SR_TXT_OBJ are utilized to replicate long texts from the SAP Script tables STXH and STXL. After deleting all entries from ESH_SR_LTXT and ESH_SR_TXT_OBJ, running the report ESH_SR_LTXT_REPLICATE in Update Run mode will repopulate these tables. Which allow to capture custom Text Id in API_PURCHASECONTRACT_PROCESS_SRV version 2, API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV to create, update Custom Text id.

2. As discussed with SAP, to facilitate the conversion of local currency amounts into USD, SAP has recommended the use of the function module CONVERT_TO_LOCAL_CURRENCY. To meet the BAPP requirement for retrieving the line item USD amount details of Purchase Orders, Purchase Contracts, and Purchase Requisitions via the APIs API_PURCHASEORDER_2, API_PURCHASECONTRACT_PROCESS_SRV Version 2, and API_PURCHASEREQUISITION_2 (OData v4), it is necessary to introduce a custom field in these APIs. This custom field will capture the line-item amount in USD, in addition to the existing local currency value.

Also need to add new filed Incoterm Location ID as it is not available in standard API_PURCHASEORDER_2
Notes from Change Ingestion:
o	There are 2 parts to the request: Purchase order creation/change and contract creation/change – 2 API’s
	For the first part, there was a standard SAP program that works for the custom text population; it will delete entries from all tables and re-populate.
	For the second part, standard SAP needs to have a custom change to send to the Boundary apps.
o	This will solve the issues with 24 objects in FUT
	Effort is low for both of them, and the work will be done for ITC1.
	FS has started

Change Title*: Add P-unit report to scope of existing object FPRXV188 – Consolidated VAT report

P-unit report is used by Indirect Tax team to self-accrue use tax on finished goods that Intel US giveaway  as samples to external US customers or use in internal processes (RND) within the US based on material standard cost. ​

Further analysis shows that one of the required data fields of the report, Material Standard Cost/unit, is not available in standard S/4 report. Hence, team is requesting to add the P-unit report as additional scope to an existing object FPRXV188 – Consolidated VAT report so the report can be custom built.
Rationale for the Change: The required data fields of P-unit report, such as Material Standard Cost/unit and Total Billback Cost, are not available in standard S/4 reports or existing data sources. No out-of-the-box solution or Fiori app can fulfill these specific reporting needs, and the legacy ECC report cannot be replicated due to system and process changes. P-unit report needs to be custom built to mitigate the risk of non-compliance with US State and local Tax Authorities and disruption to critical tax reporting activities.

Change Title*: PRMP Role needs to be sent to CHM from S4 to create Channel Partner Role in CHM
Change Description:
The Channel Partner role PRMP needs to be sent to CHM if the ZK partner number is equal to the SP partner number (i.e., ZK = SP in S4).
If the existing Channel Partner is deleted from the sold-to customer in S4, or if it is changed to a different Channel Partner, then the Channel Partner role PRMP must be removed from the customer in CRM.
Rationale for the Change: A new requirement has been identified to transfer the PRMP role to CHM from S4. The Channel Partner role should be created in CHM when the ZK partner function (IP-Channel Partner) is assigned to the sold-to customer, and the ZK partner number is the same as the sold-to number. This is an existing enhancement in ECC for integration between ECC and CHM. The same functionality must be implemented in S/4HANA to ensure consistent handling of Channel Partner role (PRMP) data between S/4 and CHM.
Notes from Change Ingestion:
o	This is an existing functionality established in ECC and CHM, but this was missed out in the design workshops.
o	Channels do not exist in IF, so it’s only needed for IP.
o	AR: Need to include the design effort and path to close – @Muppala, Praveen Kumar
o	PRMP role is not an S4 role, so MDG needs to do a lot of developments. It will not be in the data for S4.
o	AR: Include comments on why this cannot be manually maintained in S4 - @Muppala, Praveen Kumar
Notes from Core Leadership Review [Stage:
•	The team discussed a requirement from the SAP Channel Management (CHM) application involving the transfer of customer master data from SAP S4 to CHM. 
•	A specific flag, PRMP, needs to be added to the interface when the sold-to party and channel partner role are the same. 
•	This flag enables CHM to create a partner function correctly. 
•	The MDG team does not currently support this feature, so the change must be implemented in the SAP-to-CHM interface. 
•	The discussion highlighted challenges in implementing this logic on the CHM side due to its discontinued status and limited capabilities, leading to the decision to introduce the enhancement in the SAP interface. 
•	The team agreed to further explore alternatives, including the possibility of handling this logic on the inbound interface, but acknowledged the necessity of the change for end-to-end functionality
•	Decision - Need for solution is agreed on but will need to be further reviewed to identify new solution options.

Change Title*: IAO My workplace redesign:  Architecture required to allow all Intel users access to the right SAP workflows
Change Description:
Introduce My Workspace landing page on Circuit.  Build out front-end application tabs with seamless access to backend systems.  Add access control intelligence and AI enabled request process.
Change SAP user expirations from 4-month to 6-months and relocate the expiration and renewal functions from SAP to AGS, to standardize with Intel processes.
Create end-user roles in AGS to enable approver roles in SAP.  Pre-load roles with known approvers and manager/leadership approvers.
Create end-user roles in AGS to enable cross-box approvers, allowing for selected users to be present with ability to approve in multiple SAP systems.
TCM required to educate users on My Workspace landing page and system access.
Rationale for the Change: Intel Information security policy requires only users with a need-to-know be present in enterprise systems.  All Intel users have access to the existing Fiori Launchpad and can access applications like My Inbox, eCheck requests and non-SAP URLs when needed. This is possible because all Intel users on legacy SAP system are provided default "birthright" access to the SAP ECC and SRM systems. This can impact any RICEFW where an approval is needed and the complete scope is unknown. In the future, SAP users on S4 systems will not be granted any default user ID or authorization to S4 systems so default access will not be possible
Notes from Change Ingestion: Presented in Core Leadership, and it was decided that this CR will go directly to CR Meeting on Friday
Notes from Core Leadership Review [Stage:
•	Presented a redesign of the My Workspace landing page on Circuit to comply with Intel’s information security policies by eliminating birthright SAP access. 
•	The redesign aims to provide a personalized launchpad showing only the applications users have access to, with options to request additional access via AGS. 
•	The redesign includes two implementation options: an MVP costing approximately $50K and a more advanced AI-enabled version costing about $300K. 
•	The MVP approach was favored to avoid high costs and complexity, with development planned across Q4'25 and Q1'26 and backend integration handled by Deloitte.
•	Decision: Approved the MVP option

Change Title*: To automate creation of Routing & Prod. Versions with reference to SPEED BoMs for IF
Change Description: To automate creation of Routing & Prod. Versions with reference to SPEED BoMs for IF
Rationale for the Change:
In the current process alignment, FTS-IP Team had asked the business team to prepare input files manually to populate routing and production versions for materials. 
This approach was both time-consuming and susceptible to data entry errors. 
After business internal discussions, they decided to automate the creation of routing and production versions by leveraging SPEED BoMs, which were already created in the IP SAP S/4HANA system. This automation aims to improve data accuracy, reduce manual effort, and streamline the overall process.

•	To prioritize this automation for ITC1, two other already-approved enhancements (including mass upload of production orders) will be deferred to ITC2. These deferred items can be managed manually in the interim.

Change Title*: Create Zero Lead Time Order for DLCP and Non-DLCP Scenarios to convert Sorted Wafers into Sorted Dies
Change Description: Enhancement to create Zero Lead Time Order to convert Sorted Wafers into Sorted Dies to consume against Assembly order using DLCP & Non-DLCP scenarios.
Rationale for the Change:
External OSATs typically operate without distinct material master records for Sorted Wafers and Sorted Dies. In addition:
There is no standard conversion factor from wafers to dies due to variability in die count per wafer.
Materials with Dies as a unit of measure are not maintained in the system, making direct consumption against assembly orders infeasible.
OSATs do not consume wafers against assembly orders and SAP S4 System may not support wafer tracking in decimals, which leads to challenges in accurate inventory and consumption reporting.

As a result, there is a clear need to implement a solution that creates Zero Lead time order to convert from Sorted Wafers to Sorted Dies, This solution will:
Converts Sorted Wafers into Dies prior to consumption.
Enables die-level tracking and consumption against assembly orders.
Aligns with OSAT operational practices and system capabilities.
Improves inventory accuracy, traceability, and compliance with manufacturing and financial reports.
Notes from Change Ingestion:
o	Master Data has to add in die-prep items into the BOM because of this CR.
o	AR: Add in diagrams and statement that this is related to an open design topic that was just closed - @Katzorke, Matt

•	Decision: Not approved; action required to clarify and document master data impacts.
•	AR: Matt Katzorke - Clarify and document master data impacts.

•	Discussed the need to introduce a non-zero lead time intermediate production order to convert wafers into die units between wafer sort and assembly. 
•	This process requires new master data elements and production orders triggered automatically between systems (SMH to S4). 
•	The solution is limited to wafer sort UPI for OSATS and is necessary for process compliance. 
•	Master data impacts were reviewed and approved, with ITC2 handling the automated process and ITC1 managing manual orders for representative cases. The solution is expected to be integrated without major volume impact but is essential for subsequent assembly operations
•	Decision: Approved

Change Title*: Changes in COGS calculation and posting as per legal and group valuation.
Change Description:
According to the original design, the COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) calculation and posting for the FPRE0475 ( Unissued Return Credit not Accrual) were based on the transfer price (ZTPR). However, following the latest alignment, COGS calculation and posting should now be performed using the standard cost (VPRS).
Currently, COGS calculation and posting occur only for legal valuation. Based on recent discussions, the business requires COGS to be posted for both legal (Cost) and group (Cost) valuations. We need to identify the logic to capture group valuation at the material and plant levels, so it can be used for posting the FPRE0475 ( Unissued Return Credit not Accrual).

To accurately identify the COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) value for the KW00 condition in S/4HANA, it is necessary to include two additional field during the data extraction process and same will update in  custom table (ZTFPR_RET_UICP). These columns should be based on the KW00 condition requirements. The relevant Group Valuation amount for each record can be obtained by referencing the PRCD_ELEMENT and CKMLPR tables within S/4HANA.
Rationale for the Change: Requirement for Business to Recognize the  COGS at group and legal valuation level by using standard cost.
Type of Change [For Program Releases - R: 4. Change to Existing RICEFW / ECA/ Build Object
Notes from Change Ingestion:

o	We need to use a different condition type to do COGS changes.

Change Title*: GR Processing for Consignment Pickup
Change Description: The process begins with creating a consignment pick-up order in SAP to receive stock from the customer consignment hub. EDI signals facilitate seamless communication between SAP and the third-party logistics (3PL) warehouse management system (WMS), enabling automatic posting of inventory movements. This integration ensures that inventory transactions are accurately reflected in both systems, streamlining operations and reducing manual intervention. By leveraging EDI for real-time updates, the process enhances visibility and control over consignment inventory, supporting efficient stock management and timely reconciliation between SAP and the 3PL WMS.​
Rationale for the Change: LOGI0950 serves as an interface object within our system. To improve the consignment pickup process, we propose an enhancement to be incorporated into LOGE0936. This enhancement should trigger a transfer posting using movement type 309 prior to executing the goods receipt for consignment pickup. By implementing this change, we can ensure that inventory is correctly transferred and accounted for before the goods receipt is processed, thereby reducing errors and improving overall efficiency in inventory management. This proactive step will help streamline operations and support accurate tracking of consignment inventory movements.
Type of Change [For Program Releases - R: 4. Change to Existing RICEFW / ECA/ Build Object
Notes from Change Ingestion: o	Related to the VMI consignment process, and the CR is to take care of the inbound process.

Change Title*: Automate Warranty Financial Credit Posting
Change Description: Construct a program to automate the creation of a credit memos after Suppliers have approved warranty claims.  Utilizes SAP “Create Invoice” BAPI to create credit memos.  Credit memo will pass through ReadSoft for validation prior to payment.
Rationale for the Change: Current design sends Supplier-validated claims to an email address for manual processing.  The recipient of this email is expected to complete the creation of a credit memo (4mins/memo).  With an existing BAPI in SAP and the volume of current warranty claims between 5,500 - 7,000 claims per month, automation is a requirement.
Impact to Schedule: Target completion for ITC2.  This is the final step in warranty workflow, so it will not interfere with warranty testing in ITC1.

Change Title*: Inventory Reconciliation
Change Description: Change Request for an ECA Report to facilitate Inventory Reconciliation between IP S4 and External OSATs & IF manufacturing locations​
Rationale for the Change: During IP/IF/OSAT inventory management discussions it is identified to have inventory sync between IP S4 and IF & External OSATs since the transaction volume is high. It is proposed to use 7B1 PIP for inventory sync, and daily reconciliation. The data flows from IF/External OSATs to ECA via IP PDF SMH.
Added Object ID's: ECAFPRV6942, ECAFPRV6943, ECAFPRV6944, ECAFPRV6946, ECAFPRV7016, ECAFTSIFR6964, ECAFTSIFR6965
Intel ECA: ECAFPRV6942, ECAFPRV6943, ECAFPRV6944, ECAFPRV6946, ECAFPRV7016, ECAFTSIFR6964, ECAFTSIFR6965
Notes from Change Ingestion:
o	Purpose & Scope 
	The five-way reconciliation report is designed to compare inventory and financial data across five systems: IP S4, CIBR, ICOST, SAP ECC, and IF S4, especially during the transition phases of the IAO program.  
	It addresses straddle and post-go-live requirements, ensuring tight integration and identifying discrepancies between legacy and new systems.  
o	Technical Complexity 
	Major challenge is translating and cross-mapping data between systems, as SAP uses storage locations and CIBR uses operation codes, requiring complex reference tables and transformations. Manual comparison is not feasible.  
	The report will highlight discrepancies but will not determine which system is correct; manual troubleshooting processes will be developed to resolve issues.  
o	Delivery Plan 
	The report will be delivered in two phases: a three-way reconciliation for ITC2 and a full five-way reconciliation for ITC3, due to data availability and complexity.  
	Up to 10 views may be developed, with the exact number determined after further data analysis.  
	Functional specification will be delivered in a single phase by end of year, with technical specs split between ITC2 and ITC3.  
o	Ownership & Next Steps 
	Intel is responsible for both functional and technical development, with Shilpa Chandra working on the functional spec.  Manual business processes will be used to troubleshoot discrepancies identified by the report, with separate CRs for any transactional updates required.  
	The team emphasized the ongoing complexity and time required for reconciliation, even after initial report delivery, and the importance of early business team engagement.  
	Rebecca to schedule meetings every couple of weeks through the end of the year, shifting to weekly as the team moves into detailed work, to closely track progress and address any issues.
	Vanessa and team to update the object tracker as views are finalized and to provide status updates at each checkpoint.
	AR: Vanessa Clark to add notes to CR1384 that one functional specification will cover both ITC2 and ITC3, to be delivered by December’25.
o	Decision:  Approved

Requestor Sub-team*: 7.10 FTS IF - IF R2 FE Supply Planning
Change Title*: IF Memory – Model as a Constrained Material for BSM
Change Description:
As per initial design memory supply was considered as unconstraint. However, the memory going through the Back side Metallization is constraint by resource capacity​
This change will include getting PRF-RTF interaction with IP and other external customers.​
Needs to validate the overall BOM structure and resource modeling for it including coupon die supply chain and BSM related capacity constraints.​
Due to common capacity at BSM, the overall demand prioritization logic to be finalized.​
Update Solution design documents on the IF IT side..
Rationale for the Change:
The resource used for Backside metallization is shared for both memory and other FG and is a constraint resource. Hence it is critical to model the memory related products to get the effective resource availability and utilization.​

Since IF is providing service for the memory products, these should be available in CDP, and we need to have a PRF and RTF process associated with it
Notes from Core Leadership Review [Stage:
•	The CR is to incorporate backside metal constraints into memory processing, specifically for HBM. This involves updating the PRF RTF modeling to reflect capacity limitations due to backside metallization, which was previously modeled as unconstrained. The change is considered a minor extension of the existing design but requires adjustments in configuration and testing.
•	The memory design will now factor in backside metal capacity constraints within PRF RTF modeling, aligning with a prior agreement and IP’s approved portion of the change request.  
•	Adjustments include feeding capacity data from systems, configuring the BOM structure, and ensuring the product BOM is modeled through the relevant line segment. Testing will confirm proper function with the new constraint.
•	The change does not affect the overall BOM but introduces ordering by pieces instead of wafers for certain processes, requiring configuration to handle this unit of measure difference. 
•	Most application changes are estimated to take about one week, with user interface updates requiring two weeks. The BOM and MDG hierarchy need no additional effort. Testing and business process readiness are confirmed for upcoming intercept testing.  
•	The new constraint will be applied starting with ITC 2 intercept, while ITC 1 will continue to model backside metal capacity as unconstrained.  
•	Participants discussed risks of modeling many constraints, which could limit revenue by restricting orders and missing opportunities to push capacity boundaries. A balance between constrained and unconstrained modeling is necessary.  
•	The backside metal constraint for HBM will be a separate capacity corridor from fab capacity on the IP side, with ongoing communication to accompany capacity signals. 
•	Decision: Approved with acknowledgment of the challenges in managing multiple short-term constraints in manufacturing.

Change Title*: ECA Mega Tables
Change Description: Many of the developed ECA views are being created way too large. There are over 20 tables that the DARC team is consuming that have over 100 columns and a couple that have 400-500. The desire is to have optimal table structure and not large flat tables.
Rationale for the Change: Large tables cause a variety of issues: performance, development, and governance. These large tables are not even yet populated with production data and there is a high suspicion that run times will be long and cause several time outs. Development is difficult as there are duplicate columns and missing items making location the right data extremely difficult. And governing large tables are tough as we cannot see who is consuming what data and therefore no idea of what is being consumed.
Impact to Schedule: Redesigning table structure would R3 ITC1 at risk. There are downstream impacts to reporting and other systems that would need to be redirected once nee tables land. However this would have some efficiencies in our development. Also, there is still a lot of work to do with these tables as dozens of columns are missing thus leaving an opportunity to go back in and land these fields in ECA and land optimal table structure at the same time.

Link to Approval Email: Reviewed on 11/14, waiting to be updated?
Notes from Change Ingestion:
•	The remaining CRs are for FPR reconciliation reports, which have evolved from earlier releases to accommodate more source systems and updated logic. The reports aim to verify the completeness and accuracy of transactions moving between source systems (AIA, IP, ECC) and the target system (CFIN), providing summary statistics and detailed unmatched item reports with key attributes for investigation.
•	There are five main reconciliation reports covering balance reports, trial balance updates using new mapping tables, and line item comparisons for financial and management accounting postings. These reports are complex and require significant development and testing effort, recognized as one of the longest and most resource-intensive tasks from previous releases. The team emphasized the need to start development early to allow for iterative logic refinement and testing, targeting ITC 3 for comprehensive testing with stable data.
•	The estimated cost for completing all five reconciliation reports is approximately $105K covering both Deloitte Analytics and SAP functional team efforts. The reports are highly complex and require close collaboration between analytics and tower teams. Budget constraints were acknowledged, but internal absorption was deemed challenging due to the complexity. The team plans to finalize the functional design specification by mid-December to allow sufficient development and testing time before ITC 3 begins around late April.
•	A key technical decision under consideration is moving away from Power BI as the interface for these reconciliation reports, as it has proven ineffective for the required audit and reconciliation activities. The team aims to finalize the front-end tool choice by EOW WW47 in consultation with Deloitte and internal analytics teams to streamline development and improve usability.
•	Manual workarounds are planned for ITC 1 and 2 phases, with formal reconciliation testing deferred to ITC 3 when the system stabilizes and data volumes are sufficient to reduce false positives. This approach is informed by lessons learned from previous releases where early testing generated many false unmatched items due to unstable data and incomplete logic. The current state compares S4 to S4 data, which should be less complex than earlier ECC to S4 comparisons.
•	The meeting concludes with confirmations on alignment between analytics and tower teams regarding timelines, budget, and technical approaches. It is noted that while many reporting tasks could be handled internally, the reconciliation reports’ complexity justifies external support. Other reporting efforts, such as the supply chain five-way reconciliation, are planned for internal delivery to manage costs effectively.
•	Decision: Approved

Change Title*: Fine tune FICO recon report with new PC mapping table
Change Description: Incorporate the new PC mapping table with valid from and valid to date instead of the standard CFIN mapping table for PC to generate the FI recon reports
Rationale for the Change: The existing FI recon reports use the mapping table for GL and PC using CFIN mapping table. This does not have the time based valid from and valid to date . Therefore, when the report is run for the past data, the data is matched with current mapping table for PC and the results are mismatched. To overcome this short coming, a new custom table for PC with valid from and valid to is created. The report has to check the new table for PC instead of standard PC mapping table and check the records posting date within the valid to and  valid from date to determine the mismatch records. This change is to be implemented for all the FI recon reports

Change Title*: Order Change Confirmation conditions change (ZBA2, ZBA4, Z3A7) for Orders with Blocks or Status Holds ​
Change Description:
pecification (FS) includes a rule that prevents order confirmation when an order is blocked. This CR proposes modifying that rule to allow the confirmation process to proceed even during blocks or status holds.​

​

ZBA2- Order Confirmation Print Output​

ZBA4 –Order Confirmation Email Output​

Z3A7 – Significant Order change Output​

​

Note: Z3A7 triggers for intel-initiated order changes and for customer-driven (due to inbound 3A8R), trigger 3A8C be triggered. These two triggers are mutually exclusive and cannot occur at the same time.​

​
Rationale for the Change: Allowing order confirmation to be triggered even when blocks or status holds are applied ensures timely communication with customers and business stakeholders. The current restriction causes unnecessary delays in updating order status, which can lead to confusion and impact customer satisfaction. By revising this rule, the process becomes more agile and aligned with business needs, helping to maintain efficient order processing and transparency.

Change Title*:
Additional Enhancement for PTPI0470 [Payment Proposal after invoice posted from SAP S/4 HANA CFIN to Ariba]
Custom Payment Proposal Status Update for Ariba Integration
Change Description:
Currently, when we trigger a payment remittance cancellation interface, the payment proposal status in SAP changes to "Cancelled." This is an SAP bug. According to Intel’s requirements, the status should show as "Scheduled" instead, so that suppliers understand that Intel will make the payment in the future, not that it’s been permanently cancelled. The "Cancelled" status gives suppliers the wrong information to Supplier.
To fix this, we need a custom solution for the payment proposal so that the correct status ("Scheduled") is sent to Payment proposal tab in ARIBA.
Rationale for the Change: Right now, when we cancel a payment, SAP shows the status as "Cancelled." This makes suppliers think the payment won’t happen at all, but often, we just plan to pay them later. If we show the status as "Scheduled," suppliers will know we still plan to pay them. This will help avoid confusion and keep communication clear.

Change Title*: Vistex Request for splitting ITC1 Test Case Execution between ITC1 & ITC2
Change Description:
CR describes the changes to ITC Testing Approach for Vistex Rebate Automation. Vistex Rebate Automation team has 2200 Test Cases with following plan for ITC Testing
ITC1  50% Test Cases: Execute  1,100 test cases focusing on core application functionality. This initial cycle will help us identify and resolve critical defects while accounting for data availability constraints and the expected volume of issues in early testing phases.
ITC2: 60% Test Cases Execute remaining 1100 test cases and additional 300 test cases from ITC1, achieving 100% test coverage. Building on lessons learned from ITC1, we will complete remaining test cases plus additional coverage from the previous cycle.
ITC3 : 100% Intercept - execute all 2200 test cases with the benefit of an additional week & accumulated testing experience, ensuring complete app coverage.
Rationale for the Change:
Test cases need to be strategically split between ITC1 and ITC2 cycles to address environmental, timing, data constraints as listed below
1. Customer Hierarchy Limitations - Not all rebate customers and customer hierarchies may be available in ITC1 and ITC2 environments. Due to these limitations, customer hierarchy test cases will be split between ITC1 and ITC2 based on data availability. 
2. Product Hierarchy Constraints - Incomplete product master data and hierarchies in ITC1 and ITC2 environments restrict rebate testing scenarios. Product hierarchy test cases will be distributed across ITC1 and ITC2 cycles, focusing on available critical combinations in ITC1 and corner cases in ITC2.
3. Extended Test Cycle Duration –  Many test cases require extended execution time due to batch job dependencies in Vistex and across upstream and downstream system. To manage these timing constraints, long-running test cases will be split between ITC1 and ITC2 cycles. 
IQR and MyDeals System Dependencies - Deal propagation test cases dependent on IQR and MyDeals systems will be segmented across ITC1 and ITC2 cycles due to batch job timing requirements..
Vistex Internal Batch Processing Constraints - Vistex Object Test Case completion require Batch Jobs Completion which introduces delay in Vistex Specific test cases. Test cases requiring GRASP and Consumption batch jobs will be split between ITC1 and ITC2  to manage processing delays. ITC1 will cover 90% of system functionality. corner and edge test cases will be executed in ITC2.
OTC and CFIN Integration and Batch Job Dependencies - Batch jobs across boundaries such as OTC, CFIN run on a specific cadence. This will delay our test case completion in areas where financial posting is needed. Accrual and payment posting test cases will be split across ITC1 and ITC2 cycles due to the sequential nature of Vistex, OTC, and CFIN batch job dependencies. 
4. Data Set Reusability Limitations - User driven UI test cases require complex data preparation and once a test case is executed same data can’t be re-used. Consumption-related test cases will be carefully distributed across ITC1 and ITC2 cycles to preserve data integrity and avoid conflicts. 
5. High Volume of Expected Issues in ITC1  - Functional testing completed in DI0 with very limited dataset, we are expecting high volume of issues in ITC1. This will prohibit us from testing at full capacity
Notes from Change Ingestion: -	This can be tested with all of the invoices within ECC, and Vistex will be able to run testing off of this.

Change Title*: Additional logic for Credit Case Conversion in case of HLP (reference RICEFW OTCC0803)
Change Description:
Additional logic for Credit case conversion (in case of HLP) -
Check the Account Group in KNA1 table, if it is ZSPY then look at the search term field- 
If Search term field – is ‘IF HLP’ then there should be two credit cases created, one on Credit Segment 0000-Main Segment, and another on IFCC- IF Credit Segment.
If Search term field – is ‘IP HLP’ then there should be two credit cases created, one on Credit Segment 0000-Main Segment, and another on IPCC- IP Credit Segment.
If Search term field – is ‘Shared HLP’ then there should be three credit cases created, one on Credit Segment 0000-Main Segment, second on IFCC- IF Credit Segment and third on IPCC- IP Credit Segment.
Rationale for the Change:
For HLP in current system (ECC) there is only one credit segment(WWCC), however in CFIN each HLP will have a minimum of 2 and maximum 3 credit segments. (IF, IP and Main)
As per current credit case conversion design, each HLP would have converted only one credit case on the Main segment.
During FUT review for BP credit segment conversion a concern was raised that, for shared HLP the rule set is to split the credit limit as 50-50 amongst IP and IF segment. Practically the split might vary from customer to customer (eg 70-30, 40-60,etc). Creating a credit case on each segment would allow business to adjust this credit limit and will also help in keeping a better audit trail for limit assigned to each segment on BP.
Type of Change [For Program Releases - R: 4. Change to Existing RICEFW / ECA/ Build Object
Notes from Change Ingestion:
-	For high-level payers, additional logic is needed as there is no 1:1 mapping in CFIN for HLP and credit segment.
-	Manual work will be done to validate and adjust values in the recon file during MC1 prior to loading in S4.

Change Title*: Automate creation of the Repair PM Order for IMR
Change Description:
Automate creation of the Repair PM Order at IMR whenever:​ Factory technician completes Step 1 return in SIMOM​, Or EWM receives the faulty part at WH​
This removes the need for IMR to manually trigger PM order creation.​
Rationale for the Change:
In the As-Is process, when a repair PO is created by a factory (IF), the system auto-creates shop orders in SAP ME.IMR materials coordinator validates  PO against Shop order and confirm receipt. ​

The new E2E design introduces a manual “Create Repair Order” step in SIMOM/SAP PM for IMR, triggered every time a faulty part is sent from a factory site. This would trigger an interface to SAP ME for shop order creation.​

IMR would now need to perform significant manual data entry, which they have no bandwidth to support order volume(Avg. 100’s per week per lab for US labs and 10X more for Kulim labs – see back-up for summary table)

Change Title*: Delta Indicator update to restrict the duplicate records to be sent to SF
Change Description: Introduction of Delta Indicator at the GR subline level and original flag indicator at the OTC CRT and Item level table which will enable to the returns order batch job to pick only RMAs where there is any update to sublines
Rationale for the Change:
Returns Order Status Interface Batch job should send only those RMAs to Salesforce that are either new or have been updated. RMAs with sublines without any changes should not be sent to SF, which will help prevent duplicate entries, optimize the payload size, and improve overall system efficiency. To fulfill this requirement, the EWM team will implement a Delta Indicator at the EWM Subline level. This indicator will enable the Returns Order batch job to identify and select only the new or updated RMAs to be sent to Salesforce. Original Flag indicator also needs to be updated in S4 tables: ZTOTC_RETURN_CRT and ZTOTC_RETURN_ITM once any changes happens during the Inbound process. This process starts after user click on confirm button.

Impacted FS:

LOGE1314-Returns order Status Interface object to pick the RMA sublines for which CMR is generated and change the status to “Credited” at the EWM subline level
LOGE1298- Returns order Status Interface object to pick the Unexpected RMA sublines for which the price details are fetched from SF
LOGE1266 - In case of GR reversal and Inventory adjustments, when sublines are updated, status needs to be sent to SF.
LOGE1311- In case of Virtual warehouse, subline status once updated, status needs to be sent to SF.
LOGI1300- In case of CQNR interface, subline status once updated, status needs to be sent to SF.

Notes from Core Leadership Review [Stage:
•	Description: Addition of new attributes are requested to GLAP view in ECA. The GLAP view in ECA was designed to support GLAP report in DARC Hermes. Additional attributes to same GLAP report are requested to support reporting capability in FPnA and Legal IT areas. In legacy environment, the reporting to FPnA and Legal IT are support via HANA query being pulled into DARC reporting (for FPnA) and Legal IT Analytic dashboard.
•	Rational: The GLAP view was built supporting GLAP report. Downstream reporting requirements were identified later for which this CR has been created.
•	Impact to Schedule: This CR will be for ITC3
•	Discussion: 
o	These reports are evolutions of previous reconciliation reports from earlier releases (R1 and R2) but now include more source systems and require logic rebuilding. A key change is moving away from Power BI as the interface due to its ineffectiveness for this use case. 
o	The reports aim to verify completeness and accuracy of transactions between source systems (AIA, IP, ECC) and CFIN and include summary and detailed unmatched transaction reports for research and resolution.
o	There are five main reconciliation reports planned, covering balance and line item comparisons for financial and management accounting postings. 
o	These reports are complex and require early development to allow for iterative logic refinement and testing. 
o	The effort is estimated at approximately $105,000, involving both Deloitte Analytics and SAP functional teams. 
o	Testing is scheduled for ITC3 when sufficient data is available, with manual workarounds employed in ITC1 and ITC2 .
o	The complexity is mitigated somewhat by comparing S4 to S4 data rather than ECC to S4, reducing mapping and logic challenges. 
o	The team aims to finalize specifications by mid-December to allow adequate development and testing time before ITC3, which is expected to start around late April 2026. 
o	Discussions are ongoing regarding the front-end tool to replace Power BI, with a decision expected within a week.
•	Decision: Approved

Change Title*: External supplier engagement strategy post LE Merge timeline change
Change Description:
Maintain external supplier engagement strategy post LE Merge decision for pilot supplier testing starting in ITC2   1)  OSAT, SiFO   and   2)  Fab Spares
ALL other business domains have pilot suppliers starting in ITC1.
Rationale for the Change:
With the push of ITC1 timeline start for the LE Merge –
Intel  Dev/Test:    All exceptions for capabilities to be developed and tested in ITC2 have been eliminated.  All objects and interfaces are scheduled to be completed by the beginning of the new ITC1 start date.

External supplier engagement – business domain specific supplier engagement strategies have been developed and implemented focused on managing program risk (timing and efficiency) and supplier relationships.
Background:  Suppliers are already engaged in supplier owned Dev/FUT with Intel B2B teams including connectivity testing (achieves >80% confidence in external partner connection before ITC with supplier. 

OSAT, SiFO    allow pilot suppliers to engage in either ITC1 or ITC2 per alignment between business, B2B team, and supplier
Intel has pushed suppliers hard to meet the original ITC timelines.  Timeline change creates frustration, tradeoffs, and additional costs for suppliers.
Depending upon how suppliers IT is organized will drive different risk decisions:   1) PUSHOUT (new ITC2):  maintaining code for 9-12months (i.e. the code developed for IAO will be excluded and even purged from the test environment) and regression testing more work and cost   VS   2) MAINTIAN (new ITC1):  easier to not have to extend or reallocate resources.    Focus on minimizing the risk to program in partnership with supplier.  
Net result is expected to be equal to the existing approved plan assessed as low risk  OR  potentially better if some suppliers agree to move earlier

Fab Spares   H2M engagement for R3.  No change from ITC2 for pilot suppliers.  ITC1 is needed to for debugging and stabilizing new capabilities before engaging the suppliers for ITC.
ITC1 testing will conducted emulating supplier responses through E2Open.
Engaging suppliers in ITC1 increases workload to manage communication and timing of issues, fixes, and retesting for already constrained testing teams
Pilot suppliers will continue to be engaged before and during ITC1 for E2Open account onboarding and process training. (engagements are currently ongoing
Impact to Schedule: Maintains as-is strategy with OSAT/SiFO and Fab Spares

Notes from Core Leadership Review [Stage:
•	Description: Object completion Sprint to be changed to S24 as Build will be completed in S23 for Automate the PROC material master creation process based on business rules and Automate External BOM
•	Rationale: Today’s process is incredibly manual, and user needs to perform multiple steps in SPEED system to trigger this followed by additional steps in SAP system. Additionally, current process doesn't synchronize post-BOM extension updates from SPEED to SAP ECC and to avoid manual intervention
•	Impact: Not provided in CR Log
•	Discussion - 
o	A set of automation enhancements for Item/BOM/PDM/MDG data creation and maintenance had previously been approved for ITC2.
o	These automations were purposely scheduled last in the build plan because they do not impact tower dependencies and priority was given to foundational CRs (e.g., EMIB).
o	Workstreams involved include Speed, OCPLM, IBDS, and the Translator.
o	Specs are being completed in Sprint 22.
o	Build planned for Sprint 23.
o	Unit testing (FUT) would fall beyond Sprint 23, effectively becoming what the team called “Sprint 24.”
o	These automations are not needed for: Pre cutover, Data conversion, Any ITC blocking scenario
o	They still meet the new ITC1 start date even if delivered later.
o	Business may need additional headcount if automation is not available, especially to maintain proc materials manually.
o	Without automation, risks include lower data quality, difficulty meeting conversion thresholds, and increased manual workload
o	Current Build Reality: Two thirds of the work will be complete by end of Sprint 23. Only unit testing remains, needing “a little more time.” Delivering slightly late will make the program "look behind," but is id considered low risk.
•	Decision - Approved - Work can proceed, but must be tracked as late, not recast into a new sprint and delivered without affecting ITC1 readiness.

Planned Shipment Start Date :- New fields into report output (Outbound Manifest report)
Planned Shipment Start Time :- New fields into report output (Outbound Manifest report)

Change Title*: 2DN enhancement: Forecast cannot be tied to a xdock location
Change Description:
BY-CDP:
Both nodes and billing/backlog will be created at the xDock in accordance with the 2DN design.
Ensure the final CD Qty Pub is NOT assigned to an xDock location.
Ensure billing and backlog loaded at the xDock are visible to demand planners during forecast entry.

Option 1: Enhance the disaggregation logic to exclude all disaggregation drivers at the xDock location (e.g., backlog, customer/sales forecast, statistical forecast).

Option 2: Expire all nodes associated with the xDock location, while keeping measures loaded at the xDock visible to demand planners during forecast entry.
Rationale for the Change:
Demand follows revenue, and revenue is generated only from SO1 (xDock → Customer). SO2 (CW → xDock) is excluded from the transactional data feed to avoid double counting. 

As a result, nodes are created at the xDock and forecasts are disaggregated to the xDock. However, all downstream systems (FNE, ESP, OP) expect forecasts to be associated with a CW location. The ask is to identify a solution in CDP that ensures forecasts remain associated only with the CW location.

Change Title*: Static custom table in S/4 to enable Legacy (not converted) WBS MD for DPN calculation​
Change Description:
Business Requirement: Create static table in S/4 and add: 1. Legacy WBS element, 2. CEID, 3. OpEx WBS L1, 4. ECC Asset Number, 5. ECC Sub-Asset Number, 6. AMIC eligible (Y/N), 7. S/4 Asset Number, 8. S/4 Sub-Asset Number, ​

S/4 AssetECC AssetECC WBS​

Update S/4 logic (RICEFW 0883, 1650) to get legacy data from static table when Capital WBS element not found in the Asset.​

1) Asset master, Opex L1 WBS elements should be available in S4.​

2) Actual depreciation posted with point 1 asset number in S4.​
S4 Changes:
1) Need to create new custom table to maintain legacy WBS, Opex WBS, Asset...etc. 2) Hence user will maintain the data with frontend, need Fiori app.3) Additional logic to be added to existing Object FPRE0883
Rationale for the Change:
Today in ECC, 640K active assets are linked to 104K Capital WBS elements that are or will closed before the R3 go-live in May 2027. Because closed WBS elements will not be converted, the associated assets in S/4 will lack the originating WBS master data. ​

Two key S/4 capabilities depend on data fields that exist only in Capital WBS master data: CEID, OpEx WBS Level 1. These fields are required for: 1). Depreciation disaggregation from Primary → Secondary Cost Centers  2). OCOS/PCOS split logic in S/4 and downstream SAC models. (RICEFW 0883, 1650), ​

Without the WBS data in S/4, depreciation posted to these assets cannot be allocated correctly, resulting in: 1). No Primary-to-Secondary cost center split, 2). No OCOS flow into Spending and Capital models.​
Impact to Schedule: ~640K ($78b) current assets may be impacted by not having  WBS related data flowing into the S/4 Depreciation calculation logic.

Change Title*: Automate UDF Table Updates for Revised BI Sales Order Changes (Including Material Type Validation)​
Change Description:
The change enables automated UDF table updates for all revised BI-Sales Order changes (including Ship-To, RDD, quantity, and cancellations), excludes manual CBA updates, and adds validation to replace rejected FERT entries with HALB in the UDF table.​

Automated UDF table updates via the revised Build instructions (BI): Update the revised BI so all fields available in the revised BI are included in the automated updates to the UDF table.​

Exclude manual Sales Order changes from automation: Manual changes made by the CBA  should not trigger or be included in the automated UDF table updates.​

Ship-to updates flow to UDF: Ship-to updates on the Sales Order (SO) performed via 1126 should flow through to the UDF table.​

Sales Order updates ( via BI interface) flow to UDF: The following SO updates performed via the revised BI should flow through to the UDF table:​
                   Requested Delivery Date (RDD) changes​
                   Quantity (Qty) changes​
                   Cancellations ( Rejection )​
                   Adding a line (unexpected CoPo)​
UDF material validation (FERT → HALB handling): Validation step to check FERT material rejection in the UDF table and ensure the rejected FERT entry is replaced with HALB material, as applicable.​

Anafi specific fields  to be updated as Lot Type, Customer lot number, Delivery Priority, Special instructions, PO number, Item number, Required Quantity, Special Instructions.
Rationale for the Change:
Ensure the UDF table consistently reflects system-driven updates from the revised BI and SO transactions, while avoiding unintended overrides from manual CBA adjustments.​
Improve downstream reliability by ensuring key SO changes (Ship-to, RDD, Qty, cancellations) are synchronized and traceable.​
Reduce data exceptions by adding a clear validation rule for FERT rejection and HALB substitution in the UDF table.

Change Title*: Change Request to Develop a utility for mass change of production orders via load file in SAP S4 system
Change Description: Change Request to Develop a utility for mass change of production orders via load file in SAP S4 system
Rationale for the Change:
This CR is associated to create new RICEFW for mass change of production orders via load file in SAP S4 system.

Business requires an SAP S4 HANA enhancement to support mass updates of production orders using a user-uploaded template (Excel/CSV). Today, users must update production orders manually, which is time-consuming and increases the likelihood of data entry errors. especially when large volumes of orders require changes to key fields such as quantities and dates. The proposed functionality will enable users to download a standardized, filter-based template of eligible production orders, make controlled updates offline, and upload the file via a dedicated SAP program. 
The program will validate the file for completeness and accuracy, process only valid records, and automatically apply updates in SAP with appropriate error handling and traceability, improving efficiency, consistency, and data quality.

Requestor Sub-team*: 8.10 FTS IP - IP R3 Supply & Material Planning
Change Title*: Edit and Publish Single Operating Plan - Draft/Final
Change Description:
1. Run fully constrained plan in IBP and hold in BY memory for comparison to WIF scenarios.
2. Allow users to designate which IBP WIF scenario is the Single Operating Plan when it’s ready for consumption by downstream applications.
3. Provide capability to edit the output of IBP WIF scenarios used to generate the Single Operating Plan. Edit granularity should be at site, stage, item, month, quantity. Additional detail normally required for PR/PRF creation are not necessary.
Rationale for the Change:
Setting the operating plan involves generating multiple views of our demand & supply plans for executive review and decision-making.  These plans are all variations of a constrained plan. There is no single view that is pre-determined to be the one that will become our strategy going forward. Once a decision has been made, we must flag this scenario for downstream consumers as the official operating plan.  We will have a DRAFT version that comes out of the Supply Review and a FINAL version out of the Executive Review. Both DRAFT and FINAL are “published” designations that downstream systems can consume and must include all manual changes that Planning chooses to incorporate.
Planning cannot always efficiently generate the desired result through input data. Output overrides in an external layer allows planning to feed changes into reporting for a cohesive look, rather than adding footnotes for items we cannot quickly get into a scenario. (Min loading, strategic purchases, quick updates)

Related CR: This is the new business model or initiative aligned by Navid/Dave/Phil/Jasleen
Change Title*: Tester Front Office Marketplace Enablement
Change Description:
The Tester Front Office (TFO) currently supports a customer‑specific test tooling business using a centralized ordering and catalog model.
The proposed system changes enable:
     A centralized marketplace (“storefront”) for customer‑specific tester collaterals
     Standardized order intake through IFS
     True‑cost catalog pricing (hardware + labor/support) captured at time of order
     Elimination of downstream bill‑backs and manual accounting corrections
     Alignment with Foundry‑grade financial and operational requirements

The system needs to enable for customer’s order through Intel Foundry / Tester Front Office and place order to the parts/tool supplier, deliver and bill to customer based on the Intel’s catalogue price (with the true cost / mark-up).
Rationale for the Change:
This is not a greenfield build. It is a targeted extension of an existing, proven capability, designed to simplify the business, reduce financial friction, and enable scalable growth beyond the current >$300M baseline.

Without these changes, scaling this business will require increasing reliance on manual bill‑backs and accounting workarounds—an approach that is unsustainable for the Foundry operating model.

As we expand this business to additional tester collaterals (e.g., sockets, TIUs, DKMs, probe‑adjacent tooling), continuing to rely on bill‑backs will:
   Increase financial complexity and audit risk
   Obscure true product cost
   Create friction with Foundry customers and finance
   Constrain growth of a material revenue stream

Change Title*: Cal_ProdYield PDH UI
Change Description:
In the current design, users manually manage Output_YieldQty for all Output_Items in Cal_ProdYield UI. This CR covers multiple issues with the prod yield table

- Prod yield needs line items with PROC as input and output in order to correctly plan orders with PROC's

Third raised by business team

By re-designing the Cal_ProdYield UI, we can:
- Avoid the issue of having CAL_Name exceed the 50-character limit
- Allow BY to calculate intended order quantity correctly
- Maintain production yield components at correct granularity – a cleaner and easier approach

Change Title*: ProdMethod NNSD Data Source Change
Change Description:
In the current design, business sets ProductionMethod.U_ORIG_NONEWSUPPLYDATE value in Biz_Param_Control UI (This is outlined in Case 2 in the spec). If users would like to override the original value, they can do so in the ProductionMethod UI. 

However, because users can also manage Production Duration for each of the manufacturing stage in the ProductionStep UI, not only it is cumbersome for users to manage similar information at separate places, but also it creates risks for duplicating information.

Therefore, after further discussion, we decided to change the data source for  ProductionMethod.U_ORIG_NONEWSUPPLYDATE  to be sum of Production Duration maintained in the ProductionStep UI. Users can still override the value in ProductionMethod UI, but they no longer need to maintain NNSD value in the Biz_Param_Control UI. Case 2 outlined in Biz_Param_Control spec will not be needed anymore, users will use Export_Control table instead of Case 2 to define start day of the week.
Rationale for the Change:
By changing the data source for ProductionMethod.U_ORIG_NONEWSUPPLYDATE :
- Users don’t have to maintain similar information at separate UIs
- New design reduces risk for duplicated information

Change Title*: IM S4 for Capital and Construction Inventory
Change Description:
-Implement an SAP S/4HANA Inventory Management (IM) solution to support Capital Equipment, Capital Supporting Items, and Construction Inventory, replacing the legacy boundary application NIT. 
-This change enables system‑of‑record inventory management in S/4 for materials currently managed in NIT, including integrated receiving, inventory tracking, movements, adjustments, and financial posting—without deploying a full Warehouse Management (WMS/EWM) solution. 
-The scope aligns with the IAO R4/R5 roadmap and focuses on standard SAP IM capabilities to improve control, visibility, and governance.
Rationale for the Change:
-NIT is end‑of‑life and unsupported, having gone live in 1998 with no viable modernization path, creating growing operational, financial, and compliance risk. 
-Current NIT‑based processes rely heavily on manual workarounds, duplicate postings, and offline controls, resulting in data integrity issues and limited auditability. 
-Implementing SAP IM provides a right‑sized, lower‑complexity solution that retires NIT while delivering standardized inventory control and financial integration within S/4HANA.
Impact to Schedule: Request to explore adding this scope as part of R5 or Post R5, not impacting current schedule, but requires additional resources

Change Title*: Bank/Payment Screening Functionality​
Change Description: Bank master data from MDG and payment from the CFIN system will be screened in SAP GTS for Sanctioned Party List matches. Any potential hits will automatically block the associated BP or payment transaction until Compliance review is done to confirm or release the block in accordance with Intel policy.​
Rationale for the Change:
Performing Sanctioned Party List screening on bank master data and payment transactions prevents processing financial transactions involving sanctioned parties. The current, weekly, manual process is Inadequate to provide continuous coverage for all compliance risks and exposes Intel to regulatory violations, substantial fines and penalties, and transaction blocks, freezes, or other enforcement actions.  Specific gaps with the current manual process are:
*  Point in time screening only – banks screened once but not automatically rescreened when Sanctioned Party lists are updated
*  No coverage for updates to existing banks – weekly reports captures  newly created bank records but not updates to existing banks
*  No system integrated or transaction level control – screening is performed outside of GTS and not integrated with the payment process for transaction blocking if needed
The integration of Bank MD and CFIN payment txns with SAP GTS E4H  for SPL screening would close all these gaps.

The initiative addresses current limitations where serial data is handled outside of SAP S/4 and relies on manual, customer‑specific, and non‑scalable solutions that frequently fail due to data inconsistency and quality issues.​
Rationale for the Change: Intel requires an efficient, automated process to transmit comprehensive unit-level data to customers, including: Country of Assembly (CoA), Country of Fabrication (CoF), Country of Origin (COO), Date codes, Warranty information, Firmware details, Batch information​

Change Title*: Chem 3PL PIP Enhancements to Support FTZ
Change Description: Enhance the OpenText 3B12 and 2A1 PIPs exchanged with Chem 3PLs (RinChem & Linde) to support chain-of-custody for Chemical deliveries and Free Trade Zone (FTZ) zone-to-zone (Z2Z) movement reporting to US Customs and Border Patrol (CBP)​
Rationale for the Change:
2A1: the current 2A1 payload does not contain information that can be used to convert Purchasing UoM, Base UoM, to a Volume/weight UoM.  This is a problem for materials that are received in a Purchasing UoM (Ex: Case) and then distributed/consumed in a Base UoM (Ex: Bottle).  The absence of this data creates problems in Consignment settlement, FTZ zone-to-zone transfers, and 3PL fee schedules that are based upon weight of material moved.​

​

3B12: Chemical 3PLs currently deliver directly to a storage location on Intel’s campus. This process avoids a hand-off of material to the onsite 3PL, which may not be capable of handling hazardous materials and would incur additional handling charges. In SAP, this direct-delivery requires the Chem 3PL to see the S.Loc on a Stock Transfer Order (STO), which is currently not included within the 3B12. Adding the Storage Location to the 3B12 enables the 3PL to deliver material directly to the storage location without needing to hand the material off to the local onsite 3PL.​
Impact to Schedule: It is required for R3 and will be intercepted at ITC2.

Change Title*: Delivery status complete for final IP shipment Order
Change Description:
As part of detailed design and FUT activities, a gap was identified in the mechanism for IP to determine the final shipment for a build instruction, which is necessary to complete the production order in IP S/4.


As part of POLP  design, POLP will capture whether all the LOTs are complete or not for that sales order /sales order line.
OTC logistics will create the Auto DN by reading the POLP Lot  once all the LOTs are complete.  Auto DN will create the Delivery Note.
Once all Delivery Notes complete for that sales order, sales order line, sales order status will be updated as Delivery Complete( DLV); if not status will reman as partial delivery.
OTC Logistics need to enhance the current logic to put the Delivery Status Indicator in the 3B2 IDOC segment.
Once the Delivery note Pick Pack and PGI, IDOC will populate the indicator in segment; that segment will map to  3b2 Mapping.
3B2 outbound from IF S/4 to IP S/4 will carry the indicator in the payload.  OpenText will need to pass this information via a change in the current object.
IP SMH will receive the 3B2 outbound in IP SMH which will read the indicator to pass to the IP S/4.  
IP S/4 will receive the indicator and update the production order as delivery complete.
Rationale for the Change: This needs to change, else IP will not know when to close the production order for that build instruction

Change Title*: Regulatory Compliance Requirements for Malaysia/Chengdu/Vietnam
Change Description: To investigate  connections to IAO for a new boundary app to address E2E Duty Free Asset Management to enable sites functioning in Duty Free Zones to meet local regulatory obligations. ​
Rationale for the Change: Intel Malaysia through an IA Audit finding conducted a deep dive to determine systemic gaps and build predictable E2E systems for managing Duty Free Assets (which per customs definition consists of machinery and equipment, manufacturing aids, cleanroom and spares) different from Finance Asset Management. Intel Malaysia through trade facilitation enjoys significant tax exemptions to operate in 4 Legal Entities and across multiple customs zones, each with different requirements to meet.) Current systems to manage this is highly disintegrated, does not comprehend essential data and information to meet the requirements for duty free asset management across the asset lifecycles which often include reusing, harvesting and movement of goods across the LE and custom zones.. Traceability and reconciliation is broken given the complexity and scale of operations. The team has now developed an E2E framework to manage this and gotten the value decision approval from management to go build this new system which we believe would constitute a boundary app that integrates trade requirements with supply chain data and operations management. It will cover all Intel Foundry and Intel Product groups that reside in the zones. Recent alignment with Trade suggests that given Malaysia’s complexity and years of operating in Duty Free Zone, there is an opportunity to build & pilot this in Malaysia and scale this to other sites that will be required to operate in Duty Free Zones i.e. Vietnam, Costa Rica, Ohio and Arizona.

Change Title*: Bailment Scrap
Change Description: Implement a standardized, scalable solution to trace warehouse scrap material (PN/Lot/Qty) into barrels (handling units) at the time of scrapping. This includes capturing traceability data in SAP S/4 + EWM, supporting both offsite and onsite crushing scenarios, ensuring accurate reporting for customer COD, trade compliance, and internal asset recovery.

Current warehouse scrap processes are largely manual and vary significantly across sites, limiting scalability and increasing risk of errors. A unified, system-driven approach using barrel IDs as handling units enables:
Accurate mapping of lots to barrels for customer COD. 
Compliance with trade and government reporting requirements. 
Standardization across US, APAC, Ireland, and future Chandler operations. 
Reduction in manual reconciliation, emails, and human errors.

Change Title*: Convert incoming MT199 (bank acknowledgement for pre-advice files) to pain.002 format​
Change Description: Convert incoming MT199 (bank acknowledgement for pre-advice files) to pain.002 format, for allowing the status update in SAP dashboards.
Rationale for the Change: For all the pre-advice instructions - MT210s sent to the banks, the bank  sends acknowledgements in MT199 format for these MT210s which is not compatible with SAP Advanced payment management  (APM) system. SAP can only process  acknowledgements  in pain.002 format. Bank is sending  files in MT199 format since SAP will be transmitting the files MT210 – payment pre-advice format. As a workaround there is a need to convert the MT199 format to SAP compatible format (pain.002). ​
Impact to Schedule: The goal is to reflect the status of the MT210 pre-advice  acknowledgements in SAP Advanced payment management module. The volume of MT210/MT199 is 1400 in a year.
